Name: Most Holy Theotokos (Virgin Mary)
Also Known As: Panagia; Mother of God; Our Lady
Commonly Asked Intercessions: Healing; Children; Pregnancy; Childbirth; Protection from Danger
Feast Day(s): Nativity Sep 8; Entrance Nov 21; Annunciation Mar 25; Dormition Aug 15
Short Prayer (Intercession): Most Holy Theotokos, save us.
Brief Life: The Mother of God, who bore Christ. The foremost intercessor, honored above all saints.
Notes: First saint to invoke in any need. Many icons (Vladimir, Kazan, Tikhvin) carry their own commemorations.
Customs & Traditions: The Dormition Fast (Aug 1–14) precedes her summer feast, during which the Paraklesis (supplicatory canon) is chanted to her; in many places flowers and fragrant herbs are blessed at the Dormition.

Name: St. John the Forerunner & Baptist
Also Known As: St. John the Baptist; the Prophet John
Feast Day(s): Nativity Jun 24; Beheading Aug 29; Synaxis Jan 7; Finding of the Head Feb 24
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Prophet and Forerunner John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The forerunner who baptized Christ and was beheaded by Herod. Greatest born of woman.
Notes: Patron of monastics and of those called to preach repentance.

Name: Apostle Andrew the First-Called
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostle Andrew the First-Called, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Brother of Peter and the first disciple called by Christ; preached in the Black Sea region.
Notes: Considered founder of the Church of Constantinople.

Name: Apostles Peter & Paul
Also Known As: Sts. Peter and Paul; Chief Apostles
Life Experience: Imprisonment; Persecution; Torture; Repentance from grave sin
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostles Peter and Paul, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The two chief apostles, martyred in Rome. Peter the rock; Paul the apostle to the nations.
Notes: Their fast (Apostles' Fast) precedes this feast.
Works by the Saint: The Epistles of St. Paul; 1–2 Peter

Name: Apostle & Evangelist John the Theologian
Also Known As: St. John the Divine; the Beloved Disciple
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostle and Evangelist John the Theologian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The beloved disciple, author of the Fourth Gospel, three Epistles, and Revelation.
Notes: The only apostle not martyred; cared for the Theotokos.
Works by the Saint: Gospel of John; 1–3 John; Revelation

Name: Apostle & Evangelist Luke
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostle and Evangelist Luke, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Companion of Paul, author of the third Gospel and Acts; tradition holds he painted the first icons.
Notes: Patron of doctors and Christian artists.
Works by the Saint: Gospel of Luke; Acts of the Apostles

Name: St. Mary Magdalene
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Myrrh-bearer and Equal-to-the-Apostles Mary Magdalene, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: First to see the risen Christ; later preached widely.
Notes: Tradition links her to the red-egg greeting of Pascha.

Name: St. Photini, the Samaritan Woman
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Great Martyr Photini, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The woman at the well (John 4); later evangelized and was martyred under Nero.
Notes: Name often rendered Svetlana/Fotini. Principal feast Feb 26; also commemorated Mar 20 (OCA) and the movable Sunday of the Samaritan Woman.

Name: First-Martyr & Archdeacon Stephen
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Protomartyr and Archdeacon Stephen, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: First of the seven deacons and the first Christian martyr, stoned while forgiving his killers.
Notes: Patron of the diaconate.

Name: St. Thekla
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Protomartyr Thekla, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Disciple of the Apostle Paul; preserved through fire and beasts.
Notes: First woman martyr; deeply venerated in the East.

Name: Righteous Joachim & Anna
Also Known As: Ancestors of God; Sts. Joachim and Anna
Feast Day(s): Sep 9; Anna's Dormition Jul 25; Conception Dec 9
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy and Righteous Ancestors of God Joachim and Anna, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Parents of the Theotokos, who bore her in old age after long childlessness.
Notes: St. Anna especially invoked by the childless.

Name: Great Martyr George the Trophy-bearer
Commonly Asked Intercessions: Military Service; Protection from Danger; Farming / Crops
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Great Martyr George, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Roman officer martyred for refusing to renounce Christ; the dragon-slayer of icon and legend.
Notes: Among the most beloved military martyrs.
Customs & Traditions: His spring feast (Apr 23) marks the season of renewal and the movement of flocks in Balkan lands; 'Đurđevdan' is widely kept among the Serbs.

Name: Great Martyr Demetrius the Myrrh-streamer
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Great Martyr Demetrius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young officer who taught the faith openly and was speared in prison.
Notes: His relics stream fragrant myrrh.

Name: Great Martyr & Healer Panteleimon
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Great Martyr and Healer Panteleimon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Court physician who healed in Christ's name without payment; martyred under Diocletian.
Notes: One of the Holy Unmercenaries.

Name: Great Martyr Catherine
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Great Martyr Catherine, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A learned noblewoman who confounded the emperor's philosophers and was martyred.
Notes: St. Catherine's Monastery at Sinai bears her name.

Name: Great Martyr Barbara
Commonly Asked Intercessions: A Peaceful Death; Protection from Danger
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Great Martyr Barbara, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Imprisoned in a tower by her pagan father, she confessed Christ and was beheaded.
Notes: Invoked for a peaceful, prepared death.
Customs & Traditions: In the Antiochian / Levantine tradition, sweetened boiled wheat ('Burbara') is prepared and shared on her feast (Dec 4) in memory of her flight from persecution.

Name: Great Martyr Marina
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Great Martyr Marina, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young woman who overcame the devil and was martyred for her faith.
Notes: Icons show her striking the demon.

Name: Great Martyr Theodore the Recruit
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Great Martyr Theodore, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young soldier martyred for the faith; later helped Christians keep the fast.
Notes: Commemorated with kollyva (boiled wheat) on the first Saturday of Lent.
Customs & Traditions: Kollyva (boiled wheat) is blessed in his honor on the first Saturday of Great Lent, recalling the miracle by which he preserved the faithful from defiled food.

Name: St. Nicholas the Wonderworker
Also Known As: St. Nicholas of Myra; Hagios Nikolaos
Commonly Asked Intercessions: Travel; Children; Financial Need; Protection from Danger
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Nicholas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Myra famed for secret gifts to the poor and rescue of the condemned.
Notes: Origin of the Santa Claus tradition; vastly popular patron.
Customs & Traditions: Honored with gifts to children on his feast (Dec 6) — the historical root of the Santa Claus custom — and kept with special devotion by sailors.

Name: St. Spyridon the Wonderworker
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Spyridon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A simple shepherd-bishop at the First Council; worker of many wonders.
Notes: Incorrupt relics on Corfu, said to wear out his slippers.
Customs & Traditions: On Corfu his incorrupt relics are carried in solemn procession four times a year in thanksgiving for the island's deliverances.

Name: St. Basil the Great
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Basil the Great, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Archbishop who founded the first great hospital and wrote rules for monastic life.
Notes: One of the Three Hierarchs (Jan 30).
Customs & Traditions: In the Greek tradition it is St. Basil — not St. Nicholas — who is remembered with gift-giving at the New Year (his feast, Jan 1), and a 'vasilopita' is cut with a coin baked inside; the one who receives the slice with the coin is held blessed for the year.
Works by the Saint: On the Holy Spirit; Hexaemeron; the Longer & Shorter Rules; the Divine Liturgy of St. Basil; Letters

Name: St. Gregory the Theologian
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Gregory the Theologian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Archbishop of Constantinople and master theologian of the Holy Trinity.
Notes: One of the Three Hierarchs.
Works by the Saint: The Five Theological Orations; Orations; Poems; Letters

Name: St. John Chrysostom
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch John Chrysostom, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Golden-mouthed archbishop whose Liturgy is celebrated to this day; died in exile.
Notes: One of the Three Hierarchs.
Works by the Saint: Homilies on the Gospels & Epistles; On the Priesthood; the Divine Liturgy of St. John Chrysostom; the Paschal Homily

Name: St. Athanasius the Great
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Athanasius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Champion of Christ's divinity against Arianism; exiled repeatedly but unbroken.
Notes: 'Athanasius against the world.'
Works by the Saint: On the Incarnation; Against the Arians; Life of St. Anthony; Festal Letters

Name: St. Gregory Palamas
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Gregory Palamas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Athonite monk and archbishop who defended the prayer of the heart.
Notes: Honored on the second Sunday of Lent.
Works by the Saint: The Triads (In Defense of the Holy Hesychasts); Homilies

Name: St. Anthony the Great
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Anthony, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Father of monasticism who withdrew to the Egyptian desert and battled the demons.
Notes: His Life by St. Athanasius shaped monasticism East and West.
Works by the Saint: Letters (attributed)
Works About the Saint: The Life of St. Anthony — by St. Athanasius the Great

Name: St. Mary of Egypt
Life Experience: Repentance from grave sin; Temptation; Loneliness
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Mary of Egypt, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A former harlot who repented utterly and lived 47 years in the desert.
Notes: The model of repentance read in Lent.
Works About the Saint: The Life of St. Mary of Egypt — by St. Sophronius of Jerusalem

Name: St. Pachomius the Great
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Pachomius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Former soldier who founded communal monasticism under a written rule.
Notes: Father of the monastic community.
Works by the Saint: The Rule of St. Pachomius (cenobitic monastic rule)

Name: St. Macarius the Great
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Macarius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A great desert father known for humility and ceaseless prayer.
Notes: Homilies attributed to him are widely read.
Works by the Saint: The Fifty Spiritual Homilies (attributed)

Name: St. Moses the Black
Also Known As: Moses the Ethiopian; Moses the Robber
Life Experience: Repentance from grave sin; Addiction / Self-destructive habits; Persecution
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Moses, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A fierce robber who became a gentle desert father, killed by raiders.
Notes: Patron for those with violent pasts.

Name: St. Ephraim the Syrian
Also Known As: Ephrem; the Harp of the Spirit
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Ephraim the Syrian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Deacon and poet whose Lenten prayer is prayed across the Church.
Notes: Author of the prayer of St. Ephraim.
Works by the Saint: Hymns; Commentaries; the Lenten Prayer of St. Ephraim

Name: St. Isaac the Syrian
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Isaac the Syrian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Briefly a bishop, then a hermit whose writings on mercy and prayer are treasured.
Notes: 'Ascetical Homilies' widely read in monasteries.
Works by the Saint: The Ascetical Homilies

Name: St. John Climacus
Also Known As: John of the Ladder; John of Sinai
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father John of the Ladder, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Abbot of Sinai, author of 'The Ladder of Divine Ascent.'
Notes: Read in monasteries each Lent.
Works by the Saint: The Ladder of Divine Ascent

Name: St. Symeon the Stylite
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Symeon the Stylite, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Lived decades atop a pillar, drawing pilgrims and emperors for counsel.
Notes: First and most famous of the stylites.

Name: St. Symeon the New Theologian
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Symeon the New Theologian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Abbot who taught the conscious experience of the Holy Spirit.
Notes: One of only three called 'the Theologian.'
Works by the Saint: Hymns of Divine Love; Catechetical Discourses; Practical & Theological Chapters

Name: Holy Unmercenaries Cosmas & Damian
Also Known As: Sts. Cosmas and Damian; the Anargyroi
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Unmercenaries Cosmas and Damian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Brother physicians who treated the sick for free; among the chief healers.
Notes: 'Anargyroi' = silverless ones.

Name: Sts. Constantine & Helen
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Equals-to-the-Apostles Constantine and Helen, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Constantine ended persecution; his mother Helen found the True Cross.
Notes: Helen invoked by those seeking lost things.

Name: Sts. Cyril & Methodius
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Equals-to-the-Apostles Cyril and Methodius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Brothers who evangelized the Slavs and created the Slavonic alphabet.
Notes: Patrons of translators and the Slavic Church. St. Cyril's repose is commemorated Feb 14.
Works by the Saint: The Slavonic (Glagolitic) alphabet; translations of the Scriptures & Liturgy into Slavonic

Name: St. Nina, Enlightener of Georgia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Nina, Equal-to-the-Apostles, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young woman whose preaching converted the kingdom of Georgia.
Notes: Patroness of Georgia.

Name: St. Vladimir, Enlightener of Rus'
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Vladimir, Equal-to-the-Apostles, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Grand Prince of Kyiv who embraced Orthodoxy and baptized Rus'.
Notes: Father of the Slavic Christian world.

Name: St. Olga, Princess of Kyiv
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Olga, Equal-to-the-Apostles, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Grandmother of St. Vladimir and the first Christian ruler of Rus'.
Notes: Patroness of widows and converts.

Name: St. Sergius of Radonezh
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Sergius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Founder of the Holy Trinity Lavra and great unifier of Russia.
Notes: Beloved patron of Russian students.

Name: St. Seraphim of Sarov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Seraphim, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Hermit who greeted all with 'My joy!' and radiated the grace of the Spirit.
Notes: 'Acquire the Spirit of peace.'
Works by the Saint: Conversation on the Acquisition of the Holy Spirit (recorded by N. Motovilov); Spiritual Instructions

Name: St. Herman of Alaska
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Herman, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Monk-missionary who protected the Alaskan natives and lived in deep prayer.
Notes: First glorified saint of America.

Name: St. John of Kronstadt
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy and Righteous Father John of Kronstadt, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A married parish priest of extraordinary prayer, charity, and healings.
Notes: Model of the active pastoral priest.
Works by the Saint: My Life in Christ

Name: St. Nektarios of Aegina
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Nektarios, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A maligned bishop who bore injustice patiently; now a chief healer of cancer.
Notes: Hugely popular modern wonderworker.
Works by the Saint: Numerous theological works; the hymn 'Agni Parthene' (O Virgin Pure)

Name: St. Xenia of St. Petersburg
Short Prayer (Intercession): Blessed Xenia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: After her husband's sudden death she took on foolishness for Christ.
Notes: Invoked for marriage, employment, and homes.

Name: St. Matrona of Moscow
Short Prayer (Intercession): Blessed Matrona, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A blind peasant woman of great spiritual sight who helped countless people.
Notes: Among the most-visited shrines in Russia.

Name: St. Luke, Archbishop of Crimea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Luke, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: World-class surgeon and bishop who operated and preached even in exile.
Notes: Patron of medical workers.
Works by the Saint: The Spirit, Soul and Body; Essays on Purulent Surgery (medical); sermons

Name: St. John of Shanghai & San Francisco
Commonly Asked Intercessions: Travel; Children; Healing; Protection from Danger
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Ascetic bishop who shepherded his flock through exile from China to America.
Notes: Incorrupt relics in San Francisco.
Works by the Saint: The Orthodox Veneration of the Mother of God; homilies

Name: St. Paisios the Athonite
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Paisios, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A 20th-century Athonite elder beloved for practical, loving counsel.
Notes: Canonized 2015.
Works by the Saint: Spiritual Counsels (compiled, vols. I–V); Elder Hadji-Georgis the Athonite; Saint Arsenios the Cappadocian
Works About the Saint: Elder Paisios of Mount Athos — by Hieromonk Isaac

Name: St. Porphyrios
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Porphyrios, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A clairvoyant elder who counseled with love and discerned hidden things.
Notes: Canonized 2013.
Works by the Saint: Wounded by Love (his counsels, compiled)

Name: St. Tikhon of Moscow
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Tikhon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: First to organize Orthodoxy in America, later patriarch under persecution.
Notes: Bridge between America and Russia.

Name: St. Innocent of Alaska
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch and Equal-to-the-Apostles Innocent, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Married priest and later metropolitan who evangelized Alaska and Siberia.
Notes: Apostle to America.
Works by the Saint: Indication of the Way into the Kingdom of Heaven; Aleut grammar & Scripture translations

Name: St. Raphael of Brooklyn
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Raphael, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Antiochian bishop who shepherded Arabic-speaking Orthodox in America.
Notes: 'Good Shepherd of the lost sheep in America.'

Name: Holy Prophet Elijah
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Prophet Elijah, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Old Testament prophet of fire who confronted idolatry and was taken to heaven.
Notes: Hilltop chapels are often dedicated to him.
Customs & Traditions: His feast (July 20) is kept at countless hilltop chapels dedicated to 'Profitis Ilias,' a custom especially strong in Greece.

Name: Holy Prophet Moses
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Prophet Moses, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Lawgiver who led Israel out of Egypt and saw God on the mountain.
Notes: Foreshadows Christ throughout the liturgy.
Works by the Saint: The Pentateuch (the Five Books of Moses), by tradition

Name: St. Phanourios
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Great Martyr Phanourios, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A martyr whose story was rediscovered through his icon on Rhodes.
Notes: Faithful bake 'Phanouropita' when asking his help.
Customs & Traditions: A simple cake, the 'Phanouropita,' is baked and given away when asking his help — especially to find what is lost — and offered for the repose of his mother.

Name: Great Martyr Menas
Commonly Asked Intercessions: Lost Items; Travel; Protection from Danger
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Great Martyr Menas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A soldier-martyr famous for helping recover what is lost or stolen.
Notes: Major pilgrimage site in Egypt historically.

Name: St. Paraskevi of Rome
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Venerable Martyr Paraskevi, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A martyr especially invoked for diseases of the eyes.
Notes: Her name means 'Friday' (Preparation).

Name: St. Tryphon
Commonly Asked Intercessions: Farming / Crops; Protection from Illness / Epidemic
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Tryphon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young shepherd-martyr invoked to protect fields and gardens.
Notes: Blessing of fields read on his feast.

Name: St. Stylianos of Paphlagonia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Stylianos, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A hermit who devoted himself to the care and healing of children.
Notes: Icon shows him holding a swaddled infant.

Name: St. Gerasimos of Kefalonia
Commonly Asked Intercessions: Mental Health; Deliverance from the Occult; Healing
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Gerasimos, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Ascetic patron of Kefalonia, invoked over those afflicted in mind.
Notes: Incorrupt relics on the island.

Name: St. Eustathius Placidas
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Great Martyr Eustathius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Roman general converted by a vision while hunting, martyred with his family.
Notes: Patron of those enduring family separation.

Name: St. Mamas
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Mamas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A shepherd-martyr who lived peacefully among wild animals.
Notes: Invoked by herdsmen.

Name: St. Modestos of Jerusalem
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Modestos, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Patriarch of Jerusalem invoked for the health of livestock.
Notes: Blessing of animals read on his feast.

Name: St. Charalampos
Commonly Asked Intercessions: Protection from Illness / Epidemic; Farming / Crops; Healing
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Charalampos, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An aged bishop-martyr invoked against contagious disease.
Notes: Popular protector against epidemics.

Name: Sts. Cyprian & Justina
Also Known As: Cyprian the Former Sorcerer and Justina
Life Experience: Persecution; Torture; Repentance from grave sin
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Cyprian and Martyr Justina, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A former magician converted by a virgin's prayers; both later martyred.
Notes: Invoked against occult influence.

Name: St. John the Russian
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy and Righteous John the Russian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Russian soldier enslaved in Asia Minor who became a saint in bondage.
Notes: Incorrupt relics on Evia, Greece.

Name: St. Arsenios of Cappadocia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Arsenios, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A village priest-healer; spiritual forefather of St. Paisios.
Notes: St. Paisios wrote his life.
Works About the Saint: Saint Arsenios the Cappadocian — by St. Paisios the Athonite

Name: St. Anastasia the Deliverer-from-Potions
Commonly Asked Intercessions: Healing; Deliverance from the Occult; Protection from Danger
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Great Martyr Anastasia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A noblewoman who nursed imprisoned confessors before her own martyrdom.
Notes: Name means 'resurrection.'

Name: St. Irene the Great Martyr
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Great Martyr Irene, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A royal convert who brought many to Christ before her martyrdom.
Notes: Invoked for peace and concord.

Name: St. Dionysios of Zakynthos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Dionysios, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop famed for forgiving his brother's murderer.
Notes: 'Saint of forgiveness.'

Name: St. Ambrose of Optina
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Ambrose, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The most famous of the Optina Elders, sought by thousands for guidance.
Notes: Inspired characters in Dostoevsky.
Works by the Saint: Letters of spiritual counsel (the Optina correspondence)

Name: St. Joseph the Betrothed
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy and Righteous Joseph the Betrothed, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The righteous guardian of the Christ Child and protector of His Mother.
Notes: Patron of working fathers.

Name: Holy Myrrh-bearing Women
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Myrrh-bearing Women, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The women who came to anoint Christ and first learned of His rising.
Notes: Honored as a feast for all Orthodox women.

Name: St. Euphrosynos the Cook
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Euphrosynos, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A humble monastery cook revealed to be a saint in a brother's vision.
Notes: Patron of those in lowly service.

Name: St. Lazarus the Four-Days-Dead
Also Known As: Lazarus of Bethany; Bishop of Kition
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy and Righteous Lazarus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The friend of Christ raised from the tomb, who later became a bishop in Cyprus.
Notes: His raising prefigures the general resurrection.
Customs & Traditions: On Lazarus Saturday, spiced sweet breads shaped like a swaddled figure ('Lazarakia') are baked in the Greek tradition, and children sing songs recalling his raising.

Name: St. Patrick, Enlightener of Ireland
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Patrick, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A former captive who returned to evangelize all Ireland.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint venerated in Orthodoxy.
Works by the Saint: The Confession (Confessio); Letter to Coroticus

Name: St. Brigid of Kildare
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Brigid, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Irish abbess renowned for charity and hospitality.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint.

Name: St. Genevieve of Paris
Commonly Asked Intercessions: Protection from Danger; Protection from Illness / Epidemic
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Genevieve, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A holy virgin whose prayers protected Paris in crisis.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint.

Name: St. Cuthbert of Lindisfarne
Commonly Asked Intercessions: Protection from Illness / Epidemic; Animals / Livestock
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Cuthbert, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A beloved English bishop-hermit close to the wild creatures.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint; incorrupt relics at Durham.

Name: St. Bede the Venerable
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Bede, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Monk and 'father of English history,' learned and humble.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint.
Works by the Saint: The Ecclesiastical History of the English People; On the Reckoning of Time; commentaries

Name: St. Gregory the Dialogist
Also Known As: Gregory the Great; Pope of Rome
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Gregory the Dialogist, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Pope of Rome who shaped Lenten worship and pastoral care.
Notes: Liturgy of the Presanctified Gifts bears his name.
Works by the Saint: The Dialogues; the Pastoral Rule; Homilies; the Liturgy of the Presanctified Gifts (attributed)

Name: Prophet Zachariah & Righteous Elizabeth
Also Known As: Zacharias and Elisabeth; parents of St. John the Forerunner
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Prophet Zachariah and Righteous Elizabeth, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The aged, childless parents of St. John the Forerunner; the priest Zachariah was struck mute for doubting the angel and, by tradition, was later slain in the temple
Notes: Zachariah's song of praise is the Benedictus (Luke 1); their son is St. John the Baptist

Name: Martyrs Sophia, Faith, Hope & Love
Also Known As: Pistis, Elpis, Agape; Vera, Nadezhda, Lyubov (Slavonic)
Life Experience: Loss of a Child; Persecution; Torture
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Sophia, Faith, Hope, and Love, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A widow named Wisdom (Sophia) and her three young daughters — Faith, Hope, and Love — martyred at Rome under Hadrian; Sophia died days later at their grave
Notes: The daughters bear the names of the three theological virtues; in Slavonic, Vera, Nadezhda, and Lyubov

Name: St. Silouan the Athonite
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Silouan, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Russian peasant and former soldier who became a monk of St. Panteleimon's on Mount Athos; through deep struggle he received the words, 'Keep your mind in hell, and despair not'
Notes: His life and teachings were recorded by his disciple Archimandrite Sophrony (Sakharov); glorified in 1987
Works by the Saint: Writings preserved in 'Saint Silouan the Athonite' (compiled by Archim. Sophrony)
Works About the Saint: Saint Silouan the Athonite — by Archimandrite Sophrony (Sakharov)
Sources: Synaxarion; Archim. Sophrony, 'St. Silouan the Athonite'

Name: St. Chariton the Confessor
Also Known As: Chariton of Palestine; Chariton of Iconium
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Chariton, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A confessor tortured in the persecutions who survived to found the first monastic lavras of the Judean desert, shaping Palestinian monasticism
Notes: Founder of the lavra of Pharan and others; among the earliest organizers of desert monastic life in the Holy Land

Name: St. Gregory the Enlightener of Armenia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Gregory, Enlightener of Armenia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Held thirteen years in a pit for the faith, he then converted King Trdat and all Armenia to Christ, becoming the nation's first bishop and apostle
Notes: Armenia became the first kingdom to embrace Christianity as its state faith (c. 301)

Name: Righteous Joshua, Son of Nun
Also Known As: Jesus of Navi; Joshua son of Nun
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Joshua, son of Nun, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Successor of Moses who led Israel across the Jordan into the Promised Land
Notes: His name (Yeshua/Jesus) is honored as a type of the Saviour

Name: Martyrs Evodus, Callista & Hermogenes
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Evodus, Callista, and Hermogenes, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Three siblings martyred together at Nicomedia (309)

Name: Forty Holy Virgin-Martyrs & Ammon the Deacon
Also Known As: the 40 virgin-martyrs of Heraclea with their teacher Ammon
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Forty Virgin-Martyrs and Hieromartyr Ammon the Deacon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Forty Christian women of Heraclea in Thrace, taught by the deacon Ammon, martyred together under Licinius (321-323)

Name: Hieromartyr Aeithalas the Deacon of Persia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Aeithalas the Deacon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A deacon martyred in Persia (380)

Name: Righteous Martha, Mother of St. Symeon Stylites
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Mother Martha, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The pious mother of St. Symeon the Stylite the Elder (d. 428)

Name: Venerable Evanthia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Evanthia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A holy ascetic commemorated on this day; little of her life is recorded

Name: St. Dionysius Exiguus
Also Known As: Dionysius the Humble; Denis the Little
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Dionysius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of Scythia who settled in Rome, translated many lives and canons into Latin, and devised dating from the Nativity of Christ (the A.D. era)
Notes: Born in Scythia Minor (modern Romania)

Name: St. Symeon Stylites of Lesbos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Symeon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A stylite and confessor of Lesbos who suffered for the holy icons; with his brothers David and George of Mytilene (Feb 1)

Name: Hieromartyr Priscus of Capua
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Priscus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: By tradition a disciple of the Apostles, first Bishop of Capua, martyred under Nero

Name: Hieromartyr Terentian of Todi
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Terentian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Todi in Umbria, scourged and beheaded under Hadrian (118)

Name: Martyr Vibiana of Rome
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Vibiana, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A virgin-martyr of Rome whose relics are venerated in Los Angeles, of which she is principal patron

Name: St. Sixtus of Reims
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Sixtus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Reckoned the first Bishop of Reims in Gaul; by one tradition a disciple of the Apostle Peter

Name: Hieromartyr Firminus of Amiens
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Firminus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Third Bishop of Amiens, martyred for the faith (c. 303)

Name: The Twelve Holy Brothers, Martyrs of Benevento
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Twelve Brothers, Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Twelve brothers martyred in southern Italy, their relics later gathered and enshrined at Benevento

Name: St. Verena of Zurzach
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Verena, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Egyptian kinswoman of the Theban Legion who settled as an anchoress near Zurich, caring for the sick and poor
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Victorius of Le Mans
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Victorius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of St. Martin of Tours who became Bishop of Le Mans in Gaul (c. 490)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Constantius of Aquino
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Constantius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Aquino in Italy, renowned for the gift of prophecy (c. 520)

Name: Martyr Regulus of Tuscany
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Regulus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Exiled from North Africa by the Arian Vandals, he landed in Tuscany and was martyred under Totila (545)

Name: St. Lupus of Sens
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Lupus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of Lerins who became Bishop of Sens in Gaul (d. 623)

Name: St. Nivard of Reims
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Nivard, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Archbishop of Reims in Gaul (d. 673)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Giles of Nimes
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Giles, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A hermit and abbot in Provence, widely venerated in the West as a helper of the disabled, the poor, and the outcast
Notes: One of the most popular medieval Western saints; patron of the disabled
Sources: Synaxarion; Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Lythan of Wales
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Saint Lythan, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An early saint of Wales to whom two churches are dedicated; little else is recorded
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: Sts. Giles & Arcanus of Sansepolcro
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Fathers Giles and Arcanus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Monastic founders whose community grew into Borgo San Sepolcro in central Italy (1050)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Meletius the Younger of Thebes
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Meletius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An ascetic of Mount Myoupolis near Thebes, a wonderworker of the 11th-12th c.

Name: Venerable Nicholas of Kourtaliotis
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Nicholas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Kourtaliotiko Gorge in Crete (d. 1670)

Name: New-Martyr Angelis of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Martyr Angelis, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A new-martyr who confessed Christ at Constantinople (1680)

Name: Venerable Anthony of Agyia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Anthony, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monastic saint commemorated on this day; little of his life survives

Name: New-Martyr Haido of Stanos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Martyr Haido, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young woman of Stanos in Chalkidiki who chose death rather than deny Christ (c. 1820-1821)

Name: New-Martyrs Tatiana Gribkov & Natalia Kozlov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Martyrs Tatiana and Natalia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two of the New Martyrs of Russia, put to death in 1937 during the Soviet persecution
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: Righteous Eleazar & Phineas
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Righteous Eleazar and Phineas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Eleazar, son of Aaron, succeeded him as high priest, and his son Phineas was zealous for the Lord in the wilderness
Notes: Old Testament priests of Israel; Phineas is also commemorated Mar 12

Name: Martyr Mamas of Caesarea
Also Known As: Mamas the Shepherd; with his parents Theodotus & Rufina
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Mamas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young shepherd of Cappadocia, born in prison to the martyrs Theodotus and Rufina, who lived in the wilderness among wild beasts and was himself martyred
Notes: Greatly venerated in Cappadocia and in Cyprus (Morphou); invoked by shepherds and for the protection of flocks

Name: The 3,628 Martyrs of Nicomedia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs of Nicomedia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A multitude of 3,628 Christians martyred together at Nicomedia in the persecution of Diocletian (303-305)

Name: Martyrs Diomedes, Julian, Philip & Companions
Also Known As: Diomedes, Julian, Philip, Eutychian, Hesychius, Leonides, Eutychius, Philadelphus, Melanippus, Parthagape
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Diomedes and companions, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A company of martyrs who perished by various torments — the sword, fire, water, crucifixion, and hanging
Notes: Named in the synaxaria: Diomedes, Julian, Philip, Eutychian, Hesychius, Leonides, Eutychius, Philadelphus, Melanippus, and Parthagape

Name: Martyrs Aeithalas & Ammon of Adrianople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Aeithalas and Ammon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two martyrs who died under severe scourging at Adrianople in Thrace

Name: St. John the Faster, Patriarch of Constantinople
Also Known As: John IV the Faster; John Nesteutes
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch John the Faster, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Patriarch of Constantinople renowned for his strict fasting and humility; a penitential canon bearing his name long guided confessors
Notes: Out of humility he resisted the title 'Ecumenical Patriarch'

Name: St. Kosmas the Hermit of Crete
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Kosmas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A hermit and confessor of Crete (d. 658)

Name: Hieromartyr Antoninus of Pamiers
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Antoninus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An early martyr at Pamiers in southern Gaul, his relics later venerated at Palencia

Name: Martyr Maxima of Rome
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Maxima, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Roman slave scourged to death for confessing Christ with St. Ansanus under Diocletian (304)

Name: St. Justus of Lyon
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Justus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Lyon who resigned his see to live as a hermit in the Egyptian desert (d. 390)

Name: St. Valentine of Strasbourg
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Valentine, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The fourth Bishop of Strasbourg in Gaul (4th c.)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Castor of Apt
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Castor, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A married man who, by mutual consent, embraced monastic life with his wife; he founded the monastery of Manauque and became Bishop of Apt (c. 420)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Elpidius of Lyon
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Elpidius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Lyon and confessor (d. 422)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Nonnosus of Mount Soracte
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Nonnosus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An abbot of Mount Soracte in Italy whose miracles were recorded by St. Gregory the Great (c. 575)

Name: St. Hieu of Tadcaster
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Hieu, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Among the first women in Northumbria to receive the monastic habit; Abbess at Tadcaster in England (c. 657)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Agricola of Avignon
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Agricola, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of Lerins who became Bishop of Avignon and is honored as its patron (c. 700)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Lolanus of Scotland
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Lolanus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop in Scotland (c. 1034); little of his life is recorded
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: Sts. Anthony & Theodosius of the Kyiv Caves
Also Known As: Anthony and Theodosius of the Kiev Pechersk Lavra
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Fathers Anthony and Theodosius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The founders of the Kyiv Caves Lavra and fathers of monasticism in Rus'; Anthony reposed in 1073 and his disciple Theodosius in 1074
Notes: Their principal feasts are also kept on Jul 10 (Anthony) and May 3 (Theodosius)

Name: St. Theodosius of Totma
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Theodosius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Founder of a monastery at Totma in northern Russia (d. 1568)
Notes: Principal feast Jan 28 (repose); Sep 2 marks the uncovering of his relics.

Name: New Hieromartyr Barsanuphius of Kyrillov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyr Barsanuphius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Kyrillov, shot during the Soviet persecution (1918)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Hieromartyr John Ivanov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyr John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest martyred in the Soviet persecution (1918)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Martyr Seraphima of Therapontov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Martyr Seraphima, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Abbess of the Therapontov Convent, martyred in the Soviet persecution (1918)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Martyrs of St. Cyril of White Lake Monastery
Also Known As: Anatoly Barashkov, Nicholas Burlakov, Michael Trubnikov, Philip Marishev
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Four laymen martyred with the clergy at the St. Cyril of White Lake Monastery (1918)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Hieromartyr Damascene of Starodub
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyr Damascene, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Starodub, a confessor of the faith shot in the Soviet persecution (1937)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: Nine New Hieromartyr Priests of 1937
Also Known As: Euthymius Goryachev, John Melnichenko, John Smolichev, Vladimir Morinsky, Victor Basov, Basil Zelensky, Theodotus Shatokhin, Peter Novoselsky, Stephen Yaroshevich
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Nine priests martyred together in the Soviet persecution (1937)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Hieromartyr Herman of Vyaznikov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyr Herman, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Vyaznikov, martyred in the Soviet persecution (1937)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Hieromartyr Stephen Ermolin
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyr Stephen, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest martyred in the Soviet persecution (1937)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Martyr Paul Elkyn
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Martyr Paul, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A layman martyred in the Soviet persecution (1937)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Virgin-Martyr Xenia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Martyr Xenia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A woman martyred in the Soviet persecution (1937)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: St. Phoebe the Deaconess
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Deaconess Phoebe, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A deaconess of the church at Cenchreae near Corinth, commended by the Apostle Paul in his Epistle to the Romans
Notes: Honored among the first deaconesses of the Church

Name: Hieromartyr Aristion of Alexandria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Aristion, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Alexandria Scabiosa (modern Iskenderun), martyred for the faith (c. 167)

Name: Hieromartyr Anthimus of Nicomedia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Anthimus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Nicomedia, beheaded in the great persecution of Diocletian (302) while shepherding his flock through their sufferings

Name: Martyrs of Nicomedia: Theophilus the Deacon & Companions
Also Known As: Dorotheus, Mardonius, Migdonius, Peter, Indes, Gorgonius, Zeno, Domna, Euthymius
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs of Nicomedia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A company of the court and clergy of Nicomedia martyred with St. Anthimus under Diocletian (302)
Notes: Named with them: Dorotheus, Mardonius, Migdonius, Peter, Indes, Gorgonius, Zeno, the virgin Domna, and Euthymius

Name: Martyr Basilissa of Nicomedia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Basilissa, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A girl of nine who overcame scourging, fire, and the beasts under Diocletian and gave up her soul in prayer (309)

Name: Martyrs Zeno, Chariton & Archontius
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Zeno, Chariton, and Archontius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Martyrs of Nicomedia who perished by molten lead and boiling lime

Name: Venerable Theoctistus of Palestine
Also Known As: Theoctistus, fellow-ascetic of St. Euthymius the Great
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Theoctistus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Co-founder, with St. Euthymius the Great, of a lavra in the Judean desert (d. 451)

Name: Righteous Emperor Constantine the New
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Right-believing Emperor Constantine, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Byzantine emperor (d. 641) honored among the saints and buried in the Church of the Holy Apostles

Name: Right-believing King Tervel of Bulgaria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Right-believing King Tervel, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The Bulgarian ruler whose forces helped break the Arab siege of Constantinople in 717-718, defending Christendom (d. c. 721)

Name: Virgin-Martyrs Euphemia, Dorothy, Thecla & Erasma
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Virgin-Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Four virgins martyred by the sword at Aquileia under Nero, later venerated in Venice and Ravenna

Name: St. Mansuetus of Toul
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Mansuetus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Toul in Gaul and confessor (c. 350)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Ambrose of Sens
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Ambrose, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Sens in Gaul (c. 455)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Mac Nisse of Connor
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Mac Nisse, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: First bishop-abbot of Connor in Ireland; by tradition baptized in infancy by St. Patrick (d. 514)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Auxanus of Milan
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Auxanus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Milan (d. 568)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Maurilius of Cahors
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Maurilius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Cahors in Gaul, said to have known all of Scripture by heart (d. 580)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Natalis of Casale
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Father Natalis, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest of Casale in Piedmont (6th c.)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Gregory the Great, Pope of Rome
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Gregory the Dialogist, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Pope of Rome (d. 604), a monk and prolific teacher who sent the mission that converted England and shaped the Liturgy of the Presanctified Gifts
Notes: His principal feast is March 12; his Dialogues and Pastoral Rule are classics of the Church
Works by the Saint: Dialogues; Pastoral Rule (Regula Pastoralis)

Name: St. Remaclus of Maastricht
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Remaclus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Abbot and founder of Stavelot and Malmedy who became Bishop of Maastricht (d. c. 663)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Aigulphus of Lerins & Companions
Also Known As: Ayou; with St. Frugentius and his monks
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Venerable-Martyrs Aigulphus and companions, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Abbot of Lerins who, with four of his monks, was seized and martyred near Corsica — their tongues cut out and eyes plucked (c. 676)

Name: St. Hereswith of Northumbria
Also Known As: Hereswitha; sister of St. Hilda of Whitby
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Hereswith, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Northumbrian princess, sister of St. Hilda, who after widowhood became a nun at Chelles in Gaul (c. 690)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Regulus (Reol) of Reims
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Regulus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of Rebais who became Archbishop of Reims and founded the monastery of Orbais (d. 698)
Notes: Distinct from St. Regulus of Tuscany (Sep 1)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Hildebold of Cologne
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Hildebold, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The first Archbishop of Cologne (d. 818)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: Martyr Sandalus of Cordoba
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Sandalus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A martyr put to death at Cordoba in Spain under the Moors (c. 855)

Name: St. Edward the Martyr, King of England
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Passion-bearer Edward, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young king of England treacherously murdered (978) and venerated as a passion-bearer; his principal feast is March 18

Name: St. Ioannicius II, Patriarch of Serbia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Ioannicius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The first Patriarch of the Serbian Church and a wonderworker (d. 1354)

Name: Blessed John the Hairy of Rostov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Blessed John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A fool-for-Christ of Rostov who bore reproach with patience (d. 1580)

Name: New-Martyr Polydorus of Cyprus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Martyr Polydorus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young Cypriot who confessed Christ and was martyred at New Ephesus (1794)
Sources: New Martyrs of the Turkish Yoke

Name: Venerable Neophyte & Meletie of Stanisoara
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Fathers Neophyte and Meletie, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Hermits of the Stânișoara skete in the Romanian mountains

Name: New Hieromartyr Pimen of Vernensk
Also Known As: Pimen (Belolikov), Bishop of Vernensk and Semirechensk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyr Pimen, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Vernensk and Semirechensk, martyred in the Soviet persecution (1918)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Hieromartyr Meletius of Issyk-Kul
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyr Meletius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A hieromonk of the Issyk-Kul Holy Trinity Monastery, martyred in the Soviet persecution (1918)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Hieromartyrs Sergius, Basil, Philip & Vladimir (1918)
Also Known As: Sergius Fenomenov, Basil Kolmikov, Philip Shatsky, Vladimir Dmitrievsky
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Four priests martyred in the Soviet persecution (1918)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Hieromartyrs Basil & Parthenius Krasivsky (1919)
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyrs Basil and Parthenius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two priests martyred in the Soviet persecution (1919)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Hieromartyrs Andrew & Theophan (1920)
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyrs Andrew and Theophan, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two priests martyred in the Soviet persecution (1920)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Hieromartyrs Vladimir & Michael (1921)
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyrs Vladimir and Michael, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two priests martyred in the Soviet persecution (1921)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Hieromartyr Nicholas Sushchevsky
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyr Nicholas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest martyred in the Soviet persecution (1923)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Hieromartyr Euthymius Krygovich & Companions (1924)
Also Known As: Euthymius Krygovich and four martyrs with him
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyr Euthymius and companions, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest and four others martyred with him in the Soviet persecution (1924)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Hieromartyr Romanus Marchenko
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyr Romanus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest martyred in the Soviet persecution (1929)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Hieromartyr Alexis Zinoviev
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyr Alexis, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest martyred in the Soviet persecution (1937)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Hieromartyr Elias Bazhanov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyr Elias, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest martyred in the Soviet persecution (1937)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Hieromartyr Peter Sorokin
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyr Peter, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A deacon of Alma-Ata who died a confessor of the faith (1953)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: Blessed Peter of Uglich, Fool-for-Christ
Short Prayer (Intercession): Blessed Peter, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest of Uglich who took up the way of foolishness-for-Christ (d. 1866)

Name: Holy Prophet Moses the God-seer
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Prophet Moses, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The God-seer who led Israel out of Egypt, received the Law on Mount Sinai, and beheld the Promised Land from Mount Nebo
Notes: Also commemorated on the Sunday of the Holy Forefathers and, with Elijah and Elisha, on Jul 20

Name: Martyrs Thathuil & Bebaia of Edessa
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Thathuil and Bebaia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A former pagan priest of Edessa converted by Bishop Barsimaeus, blinded and tortured, and his companion, martyred under Hadrian (c. 116)

Name: Martyr Sarbellus of Edessa
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Sarbellus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A pagan high priest of Edessa who, converted to Christ, was martyred by stoning (also commemorated Jan 29)

Name: Martyr Hermione of Ephesus
Also Known As: Hermione, daughter of Philip the Deacon
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Hermione, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A daughter of the Apostle Philip the Deacon, gifted in healing and prophecy, martyred at Ephesus (c. 117)

Name: Sts. Theotimos & Theodoulos
Also Known As: the executioners converted by St. Hermione
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Theotimos and Theodoulos, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two executioners who came to faith in Christ through St. Hermione and reposed in peace (c. 117)

Name: St. Petronius of Ephesus
Also Known As: Petronius, disciple of St. John the Theologian
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Father Petronius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple, likely of St. John the Theologian, who met St. Hermione at Ephesus (2nd c.)

Name: Martyrs Christodoula & Her Sons
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Christodoula and her sons, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A mother martyred with her three sons — Urban, Prilidian, and Epolonios (251)

Name: Hieromartyr Babylas of Antioch
Also Known As: Babylas, Bishop of Antioch, with three youths
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Babylas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Antioch who barred an emperor stained with blood from the church; martyred with three young students, Ammonius, Donatos, and Faustus (c. 251)

Name: Martyr Babylas of Nicomedia & 84 Children
Also Known As: Babylas the teacher and his pupils
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Babylas and the children with you, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian teacher martyred at Nicomedia together with his eighty-four young pupils

Name: Martyrs Theodore, Ammianus, Julian, Oceanus & Centurionus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Theodore and companions, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Martyrs of Nicomedia whose feet were cut off and who were cast into the fire under Maximian (288)

Name: Martyrs Kegourus, Secundinus, Secundus & Their Mother Jerusalem
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs and their mother Jerusalem, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A mother and her sons martyred at Veria (Berea) in Macedonia

Name: Martyr Charitina of Amisus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Charitina, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A virgin-martyr of Amisus on the Black Sea (c. 304); her principal feast is October 5

Name: St. Petronius of Egypt
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Petronius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of St. Pachomius the Great who briefly led the Egyptian communities after him (346)

Name: St. Candida the Elder of Naples
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Candida, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An aged woman of Naples who welcomed the Apostle Peter, was healed and baptized by him, and led St. Aspren to the faith (c. 78)

Name: Martyr Marcellus of Chalon
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Marcellus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest who, refusing a pagan banquet, was buried alive to the waist by the Saône and praised God for three days until he died (c. 178)

Name: St. Marinus of San Marino
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Marinus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Dalmatian stonemason and deacon who lived as a hermit; the Republic of San Marino grew around his cell (4th c.)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: Hieromartyr Marcellus of Trier
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Marcellus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of Trier venerated as a martyr

Name: St. Salvinus of Verdun
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Salvinus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The third Bishop of Verdun in northern Gaul (c. 420)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Boniface I, Pope of Rome
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Boniface, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Roman priest elected Pope of Rome in 418 (d. 422)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Monessa of Ireland
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Monessa, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Irish chieftain's daughter converted by St. Patrick, who reposed in joy soon after her baptism (456)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Caletricus of Chartres
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Caletricus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Chartres in Gaul (c. 580)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Ultan of Ardbraccan
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Ultan, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Irish bishop renowned for his love of children, gathering and caring for orphans (7th c.)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Rhuddlad of Anglesey
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Rhuddlad, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Patron saint of Llanrhyddlad in Anglesey, Wales (7th c.)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Ida of Herzfeld
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Ida, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A noblewoman who, widowed young, founded the convent of Herzfeld in Westphalia and fed the poor daily from a stone coffin she had made for herself (c. 813)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: Hieromartyr Sulpicius of Bayeux
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Sulpicius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Bayeux, slain by Viking raiders at Livry (843)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Symeon of Gareji
Also Known As: Symeon the Wonderworker of David Gareja
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Symeon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Abbot and wonderworker of the David Gareja monastery in Georgia (1773)

Name: Venerable Anthimus the Blind of Cephalonia
Also Known As: Anthimus the New Ascetic; patron of Astypalaia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Anthimus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A blind ascetic of Cephalonia, founder of a monastery and patron of Astypalaia (1782)

Name: New Hieromartyr Parthenius of Kiziltash
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyr Parthenius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Abbot of the Kiziltash Monastery in the Crimea, slain for the faith (1867)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Hieromartyr Gregory of Schlusselburg
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyr Gregory, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Schlüsselburg, martyred in the Soviet persecution (1937)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Hieromartyr Sergius of Narva
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyr Sergius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Narva, martyred in the Soviet persecution (1937)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Hieromartyr Stephen Kuskov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyr Stephen, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A hieromonk of Nikolskoye in Tver, martyred in the Soviet persecution (1937)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: Ten New Hieromartyr Priests of 1937
Also Known As: Paul Vasilievsky, John Vasilevsky, Nicholas Lebedev, Nicholas Sretensky, John Romashkin, Nicholas Voshtev, Alexander Nikolsky, Peter Lebedinsky, Michael Bogorodsky, Elias Izmailov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Ten priests martyred together in the Soviet persecution (1937)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Martyrs Basil, Peter, Stephen & Alexander (1937)
Also Known As: Basil Yezhov, Peter Lonskov, Stephen Mityushkin, Alexander Blokhin
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Four laymen martyred in the Soviet persecution (1937)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Hieromartyr Peter of Dabro-Bosnia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyr Peter, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Metropolitan of Dabro-Bosnia, seized and martyred during the persecutions of the Second World War (1941)
Notes: Among the Serbian New Martyrs

Name: New Martyr Elena Chernova
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Martyr Elena, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A woman martyred for the faith during the Soviet persecution (1942)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: St. Ioasaph, Bishop of Belgorod
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Ioasaph, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A beloved bishop of Belgorod (d. 1754), known for almsgiving and strictness of life; his relics were uncovered on this day in 1911
Notes: His repose is commemorated on Dec 10

Name: New-Martyr Theodore Hatzis of Mytilene
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Martyr Theodore, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A new-martyr of Mytilene who confessed Christ under the Ottomans (1784)

Name: Virgin-Martyr Rhais of Alexandria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Rhais, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young woman of Alexandria who, seeing captive Christians, openly confessed Christ and was martyred with them (c. 308)

Name: Martyrs Juventinus & Maximinus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Juventinus and Maximinus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two soldiers of the imperial guard martyred at Antioch for rebuking the apostate emperor Julian (361-363)

Name: Martyrs Urban, Theodore, Medimnus & 77 Companions
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Urban, Theodore, Medimnus, and companions, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Seventy-seven priests and deacons of Nicomedia put on a ship and burned at sea by command of the Arian emperor Valens (370)

Name: Hieromartyr Abdas of Persia & Companions
Also Known As: Abdias; Obadiah, with Hormizd and Sunin
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Abdas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop in Persia martyred with his companions in the persecution of the Church of the East (c. 420)

Name: Martyr Herculanus of Porto
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Herculanus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A soldier martyred at Porto near Rome, probably under Marcus Aurelius (c. 180)

Name: Martyrs Quintius, Arcontius & Donatus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Quintius, Arcontius, and Donatus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Martyrs venerated at Capua and elsewhere in southern Italy

Name: St. Obdulia of Toledo
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Obdulia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A holy virgin venerated at Toledo in Spain

Name: St. Genebald of Laon
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Genebald, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of Laon and kinsman of St. Remigius who did seven years of penance for a grave sin (c. 555)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Victorinus of Como
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Victorinus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Como in Italy and an ardent opponent of Arianism (644)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Bertin of Sithiu
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Bertin, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of Luxeuil who became abbot of Sithiu in Gaul and founded many monasteries (c. 709)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: Passion-bearers Boris & Gleb
Also Known As: Romanus and David, princes of Rus'
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Passion-bearers Boris and Gleb, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The first saints of Rus', princes who chose to be killed rather than take up arms against their brother (1015); Gleb (David) was slain on this day
Notes: Their joint feast is Jul 24; the translation of their relics is May 2

Name: Venerable-Martyr Athanasius of Brest
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Venerable-Martyr Athanasius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Abbot of Brest who opposed the forced union of the Church and was tortured and shot for the Orthodox faith (1648)

Name: New Hieromartyr Alexis of Great Ustiug
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyr Alexis, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Archbishop of Great Ustiug, a confessor who suffered in the Soviet persecution (1937)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Martyr Euthymius Kochev
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Martyr Euthymius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A martyr of the Soviet persecution (1937)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Martyrs of the Czech Lands
Also Known As: Vladimír Petřek, Václav Čikl, Ján Sonnevend, Václav Ornest
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Martyrs of the Czech Lands, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Clergy and laymen of the cathedral of Ss. Cyril and Methodius in Prague, martyred by the Nazis in 1942 for sheltering the persecuted

Name: Martyrs Romulus & the 11,000 in Armenia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Romulus and companions, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Roman officer and a great multitude of soldiers martyred in Armenia for confessing Christ (c. 107-115)

Name: Martyrs Cyriacus, Faustus the Priest, Abibus the Deacon & Companions
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs of Alexandria, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest, a deacon, and eleven others beheaded for Christ at Alexandria under Decius (250)

Name: Martyrs of Egypt: Calodote, Macarius & Companions
Also Known As: Macarius, Andrew, Cyriacus, Dionysius, Andrew the Soldier, Andropelagia, Thecla, Theoctistus, Sarapabon
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs of Egypt, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A company of men and women, among them a soldier and a senator, martyred together in Egypt (256)

Name: Hieromartyr Cyril of Gortyna
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Cyril, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The aged Bishop of Gortyna on Crete, beheaded for Christ (c. 303)

Name: Martyrs Eudoxius, Zeno, Macarius & 1,104 Soldiers
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Eudoxius and companions, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An officer and a great number of soldiers martyred at Melitene in the persecution of Diocletian (311-312)

Name: St. Archippus of Hierapolis
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Archippus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A pious ascetic who tended the shrine of the Archangel Michael at Chonae (Colossae)

Name: St. David of Hermopolis
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father David, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A robber-chief in Egypt who repented utterly and became a monk of great holiness (6th c.)

Name: Martyrs Augustine, Sanctian & Beata
Also Known As: born in Spain, martyred near Sens
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Augustine, Sanctian, and Beata, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born in Spain, they fled persecution and were martyred near Sens in Gaul (273)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Petronius of Verona
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Petronius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Verona in Italy and confessor (c. 450)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Arator of Verdun
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Arator, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The fourth Bishop of Verdun in Gaul (c. 460)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Maccallin of Lusk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Maccallin, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Lusk in Ireland, also venerated in Scotland (c. 497)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: African Confessors exiled by the Vandals
Also Known As: Donatian, Praesidius, Mansuetus, Germanus, Fusculus, Laetus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Confessors and Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: African bishops scourged and driven into exile by the Arian Vandal king Huneric for confessing the true faith; among them Laetus was burned alive (5th c.)

Name: St. Eleutherius of Spoleto
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Eleutherius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Abbot of St. Mark's at Spoleto and later a monk in Rome, a wonderworker praised by St. Gregory the Great (c. 590)

Name: St. Faustus of Syracuse
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Faustus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Abbot of the monastery of St. Lucy in Syracuse, Sicily (c. 607)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Cagnoald of Laon
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Cagnoald, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of Luxeuil, brother of St. Faro and St. Burgundofara, who became Bishop of Laon (c. 635)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Beya of Copeland
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Beya, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Irish virgin who founded a convent in Cumbria; the place of St. Bees is named for her (7th c.)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: Martyrs Felix & Augebert
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Felix and Augebert, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two Englishmen, once slaves ransomed by St. Gregory the Great and prepared for mission, killed by pagans in Champagne before they could preach (7th c.)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Magnus of Fussen
Also Known As: Magnoaldus; Mang; Enlightener of the Allgau
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Magnus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Irish monk who preached with Sts. Columbanus and Gall and enlightened the Allgau region of Germany, founding the monastery of Fussen (8th c.)

Name: New Hieromartyr Maximus Sandovich
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyr Maximus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest of the Lemko people of the Carpathians, imprisoned and shot for his Orthodox faith (1914)
Notes: Of Lemkovina (Carpatho-Rus'); among the Carpatho-Russian saints

Name: New Hieromartyr Demetrius Spassky
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyr Demetrius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest martyred in the Soviet persecution (1918)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Hieromartyrs John Pavlovsky & Vsevolod Poteminsky
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyrs John and Vsevolod, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two priests martyred in the Soviet persecution (1937)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Hieromartyr Constantine Bogoslovsky
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyr Constantine, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest martyred in the Soviet persecution (1937)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: Apostles Evodius & Onesiphorus
Also Known As: Euodias and Onesiphorus of the Seventy
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostles Evodius and Onesiphorus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two of the Seventy: Evodius, second bishop of Antioch, who first called the disciples 'Christians'; and Onesiphorus, praised by the Apostle Paul for refreshing him in prison

Name: Martyr Eupsychios of Caesarea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Eupsychios, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A martyr of Caesarea in Cappadocia who, on release from prison, sold his estate and gave to the poor and even to his accusers, then was seized again and slain (c. 130)

Name: Hieromartyr Stephen, Pope of Rome
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Stephen, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Pope of Rome martyred with his companions in the persecution of Valerian (257)

Name: Martyr Sozon of Cilicia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Sozon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A shepherd of Cilicia who broke a golden idol to give to the poor and was burned for Christ under Maximian (304)

Name: Martyr Sozon of Cyprus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Sozon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A martyr of Cyprus whose relics are kept at the monasteries of Machairas and Chrysorrogiatissa (7th c.)

Name: Venerable Kassiani the Hymnographer
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Kassiani, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A noble Byzantine nun, abbess, and the first woman whose hymns entered the Church's services; a confessor in the time of the iconoclasts
Notes: Her hymn on the sinful woman is chanted on Holy Wednesday; patron of Kasos
Customs & Traditions: Hymns and verses, including the Troparion of Kassiani

Name: Venerable Peter the Cappadocian
Also Known As: Peter of the Monastery of the Deep Stream
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Peter, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Second abbot of the Monastery of the Deep Stream at Triglia near Constantinople (10th c.)

Name: Venerable Luke of Lycaonia
Also Known As: Luke of the Monastery of the Deep Stream
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Luke, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Third abbot of the Monastery of the Deep Stream at Triglia near Constantinople (after 975)

Name: Virgin-Martyr Regina of Autun
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Regina, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A virgin-martyr of Autun in Gaul who endured prison, the rack, and fire before death (c. 286)

Name: Martyr Anastasius the Fuller
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Anastasius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A fuller who painted a cross on his door to confess Christ openly at Salona and was drowned for the faith (304)

Name: St. Evortius of Orleans
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Evortius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A subdeacon of the Roman Church chosen Bishop of Orleans in Gaul (c. 340)

Name: St. Pamphilus of Capua
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Pamphilus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Greek by birth who became Bishop of Capua in Italy (c. 400)

Name: Virgin-Martyr Grimonia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Grimonia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Irish virgin martyred in Picardy in Gaul in defense of her chastity (4th c.)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Augustalis of Arles
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Augustalis, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Arles in Gaul and confessor (c. 450)

Name: St. Memorius & Companions
Also Known As: Nemorius the Deacon and five companions
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Memorius and companions, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A deacon of Troyes sent with five companions to plead with Attila, who had them all beheaded (451)

Name: St. Gratus of Aosta
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Gratus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Aosta in Italy, of which he is the patron saint (c. 470)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Carissima of Albi
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Carissima, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An anchoress in a forest near Albi in Gaul, later of the convent of Vious (5th c.)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Cloud of Nogent
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Cloud, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Frankish prince, grandson of King Clovis, who renounced the throne to become a priest and hermit and founded the monastery of Nogent near Paris (560)

Name: St. Balin
