Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Balin, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An English-born saint who followed St. Colman to Iona and settled in Connaught in Ireland (7th c.)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Madalberta of Maubeuge
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Madalberta, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Daughter of holy parents, she became a nun and Abbess of Maubeuge in Gaul (706)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Hilduard
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Hilduard, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Founder of the monastery of St. Peter at Dickelvenne on the Scheldt in Belgium (c. 750)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Alchmund of Hexham
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Alchmund, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The seventh Bishop of Hexham in England, renowned for holiness and pastoral care (781)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Tilbert of Hexham
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Tilbert, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Hexham in Northumbria in England (789)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Faciolus of Poitiers
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Faciolus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk at the monastery of St. Cyprian in Poitiers in Gaul (c. 950)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Gosslin of Toul
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Gosslin, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Toul in Gaul, a builder of churches and helper of the poor (962)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Theoderic of Metz
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Theoderic, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Metz in Germany (984)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. John of Novgorod
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Archbishop and wonderworker of Novgorod under whom the city was delivered through the Sign icon of the Theotokos (1186)

Name: Sts. Alexander Peresvet & Andrew Oslyabya
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Fathers Alexander and Andrew, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two monks blessed by St. Sergius of Radonezh who fought and fell at the Battle of Kulikovo; Peresvet opened the battle in single combat (1380)

Name: Sts. Symeon & Amphilochius of Pangarati
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Fathers Symeon and Amphilochius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Abbots of the Pângărați Monastery in Romania (15th-16th c.)

Name: Venerable Serapion of Pskov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Serapion, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Spaso-Eleazar Monastery near Pskov (1480)

Name: Hieromartyr Macarius of Kanev
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Macarius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An archimandrite of Pinsk and Kanev slain by raiding Tatars while at prayer (1678)

Name: St. Macarius of Optina
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Macarius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: One of the great Elders of Optina, a guide of souls who oversaw the publishing of the patristic writings (1860)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of the Optina Elders

Name: Venerable Daniel of Katounakia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Daniel, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A renowned ascetic elder of Katounakia on Mount Athos (1929)

Name: New Hieromartyrs Peter Sneznitsky & Michael Tikhonitsky
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyrs Peter and Michael, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two priests martyred in the Soviet persecution (1918)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Hieromartyr Alexander the Deacon
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyr Alexander, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A deacon martyred in the Soviet persecution (1918)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Hieromartyr John Maslovsky
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyr John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest of the Amur region martyred in the Soviet persecution (1921)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Hieromartyr Eugene of Nizhny Novgorod
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyr Eugene, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Metropolitan of Nizhny Novgorod, a long-suffering confessor shot in the Soviet persecution (1937)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Hieromartyr Leo of St. Alexander Nevsky Lavra
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyr Leo, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An archimandrite of the St. Alexander Nevsky Lavra martyred in the Soviet persecution (1937)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Hieromartyr Nicholas of Selinginsk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyr Nicholas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Abbot of the Holy Trinity Selinginsk Monastery, martyred in the Soviet persecution (1937)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Hieromartyr Eugene of Zhitomir
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyr Eugene, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An abbot of Zhitomir in Ukraine, martyred in the Soviet persecution (1937)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Hieromartyr Pachomius of Skanov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyr Pachomius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A hieromonk of the Holy Trinity Skanov Monastery near Penza, martyred in the Soviet persecution (1937)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Hieromartyr Stephen of Robchik
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyr Stephen, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest of Robchik in the Bryansk region, martyred in the Soviet persecution (1937)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Hieromartyr Gregory Averin
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyr Gregory, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest of Ivanovo martyred in the Soviet persecution (1937)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Hieromartyr Basil Sungurov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyr Basil, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest martyred in the Soviet persecution (1937)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: St. Anastasius II, Pope of Rome
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Anastasius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Pope of Rome at the close of the fifth century (498)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Kingsmark
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Kingsmark, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of St. Dyfrig, by tradition from Scotland but honored in Wales (5th c.)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Aethelburh of Kent
Also Known As: Ethelburga of Kent; Abbess of Lyminge
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Mother Aethelburh, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian princess of Kent whose marriage to King Edwin helped bring the faith to Northumbria; in widowhood she founded and led the convent of Lyminge (c. 647)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Disibod
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Disibod, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Irish-born monk who went with companions to Germany and founded the monastery on the hill later called Disibodenberg (c. 700)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Sergius I, Pope of Rome
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Sergius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Of Syrian descent, born in Sicily; Pope of Rome who blessed and fostered the English missions in Frisia and Germany (701)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Corbinian
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Corbinian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Frankish hermit consecrated bishop and sent to preach in Bavaria, settling at Freising (c. 730)

Name: Sts. Ine of Wessex & Queen Ethelburga
Also Known As: Ina, King of Wessex, and his wife Ethelburga
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Ine and Ethelburga, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A king of Wessex, a wise lawgiver and restorer of Glastonbury, who with his queen abdicated and went to Rome to end their days in devotion; he founded the English hospice there (c. 740)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Lucian of Alexandrov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Lucian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Abbot of the Alexandrov community in Russia (1654)

Name: New Martyr Athanasius of Thessaloniki
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Martyr Athanasius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A new-martyr who confessed Christ under the Ottomans at Thessaloniki (1774)

Name: St. Sophronius of Achtaleia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Sophronius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Achtaleia in the kingdom of Iberia (Georgia) (1803)

Name: Sts. Ioane & Giorgi-Ioane of Georgia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Confessors Ioane and Giorgi-Ioane, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two archimandrites of Georgia who kept the monastic life and confessed the faith through the Soviet years (1957 and 1960)

Name: Venerable Theophanes the Confessor & Faster
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Theophanes, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A confessor and great faster of Mount Diabenos (299)

Name: Martyr Chariton
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Chariton, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A martyr beheaded for Christ

Name: Martyr Severian of Sebaste
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Severian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A soldier who visited the Forty Martyrs of Sebaste in prison and was himself tortured and martyred for Christ (320)

Name: St. Rufus of Thessaloniki
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Rufus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Thessaloniki (d. 434)

Name: St. Agathocles of Koroni
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Agathocles, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Koroni and one of the fathers of the Third Ecumenical Council (5th c.)

Name: Martyrs Hyacinth, Alexander & Tiburtius
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Hyacinth, Alexander, and Tiburtius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Martyrs of the Sabine country in Italy, about thirty miles from Rome

Name: St. Kieran of Clonmacnoise
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Kieran, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: One of the Twelve Apostles of Ireland and first abbot of Clonmacnoise, who gave his monks an ascetic rule (c. 545)

Name: St. Osmanna of Brittany
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Osmanna, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Irish virgin who left for Brittany and lived as an anchoress near Saint-Brieuc (c. 650)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Omer of Therouanne
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Omer, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of Luxeuil who as Bishop of Therouanne covered the region with monasteries; the town of Saint-Omer grew around his foundation (670)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Bettelin of Crowland
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Bettelin, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A hermit of Crowland and disciple of St. Guthlac (8th c.)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Wulfhilda of Barking
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Mother Wulfhilda, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Abbess of Barking and Horton in England, expelled and later restored, whose relics were found incorrupt (c. 1000)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: Blessed Nicetas the Hidden
Short Prayer (Intercession): Blessed Nicetas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A saint of Constantinople who hid his great virtue beneath an ordinary life (12th c.)

Name: Venerable Joseph of Volokolamsk
Also Known As: Joseph of Volotsk; Joseph the Wonderworker
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Joseph, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Founder of the Volokolamsk Monastery, a defender of the faith against heresy and a great organizer of cenobitic monastic life (1515)
Notes: His feast is also kept on Oct 18

Name: Venerable Joachim of Opochka
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Joachim, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Founder of the Opochka Monastery near Pskov (c. 1550)

Name: St. Joachim of Shartomsk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Joachim, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the St. Nicholas-Shartomsk Monastery in Suzdal (1625)

Name: St. Cyriacus of Tazlau
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Cyriacus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Tazlau Monastery in Moldavia (1660)

Name: Venerable Onuphrius of Vorona
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Onuphrius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An ascetic of Vorona in Moldavia (1789)

Name: New Hieromartyrs Gregory Garyev & Alexander Ipatov
Also Known As: Gregory Garyev, Priest, and Alexander Ipatov, Deacon
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyrs Gregory and Alexander, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest and a deacon martyred in the Soviet persecution (1918)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Hieromartyr Zachariah of Voronezh
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyr Zachariah, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Archbishop of Voronezh, martyred in the Soviet persecution (1937)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Hieromartyrs Sergius, Joseph & Alexis
Also Known As: Sergius Uklonsky, Joseph Arkharov, Alexis Uspensky, Priests
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Three priests martyred in the Soviet persecution (1937)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Hieromartyr Dmitry Troitsky
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyr Dmitry, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A deacon martyred in the Soviet persecution (1937)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Martyr Basil Shikalov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Martyr Basil, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A layman martyred in the Soviet persecution (1937)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Hieromartyr Andronicus Surikov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyr Andronicus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A hieromonk of Moscow martyred in the Soviet persecution (1938)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Hieromartyr Alexander Vinogradov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyr Alexander, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest martyred in the Soviet persecution (1942)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: St. Theodosius of Chernigov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Theodosius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A beloved archbishop of Chernigov (d. 1696) and wonderworker, whose relics were uncovered on this day in 1896
Notes: His repose is commemorated on Feb 5

Name: Synaxis of the Glinsk Elders
Also Known As: Vasily Kishkin, Philaret Danilovsky, Theodoty Levchenko, Makary Sharov, Martiry Kirichenko, Evfimy Lyubimchenko, Dosithy Kolchenkov, Iliodor Golovatsky, Innokenty Stepanov, Luke Shvets, Archippus Shestakov, Ioanniky Gomolko, Seraphim Amelin, Andronik Lukash, Seraphim Romantsov, Zinovy Mazhuga
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Elders of Glinsk, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The revered spiritual elders of the Glinsk Hermitage, masters of the prayer of the heart and guides of countless souls
Notes: Elder Makarios (Sharov) of Glinsk (1802-1864) is individually commemorated Feb 21.

Name: Holy Apostles Apelles, Lucius & Clement
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostles, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Three of the Seventy: Apelles of Heraklion, Lucius of Laodicea in Syria, and Clement of Sardica (1st c.)

Name: Martyr Barypsabas
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Barypsabas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A hermit from the East martyred in Dalmatia (2nd c.)

Name: Martyrs Menodora, Metrodora & Nymphodora
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Menodora, Metrodora, and Nymphodora, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Three virgin sisters of Bithynia who lived in ascetic seclusion and were crowned together in martyrdom under Maximian (305-311)

Name: St. Eudokia the Child
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Eudokia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A holy young girl whose memory is preserved in the Jerusalem Canonarion

Name: St. Pulcheria the Empress
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Empress Pulcheria, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Byzantine empress who vowed virginity, guided the empire with wisdom and piety, and championed the Orthodox faith at the Council of Chalcedon, building many churches to the Theotokos (453)

Name: Venerable Peter & Paul of Nicaea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarchs Peter and Paul, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two bishops of Nicaea, confessors of the faith (9th c.)

Name: Martyrs of Sigum: Nemesian & Companions
Also Known As: Nemesian, Felix, Lucius, Felix, Litteus, Polyanus, Victor, Jader, Dativus and companions
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Confessors and Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishops and clergy of Numidia condemned to the marble quarries of Sigum under Valerian, to whom St. Cyprian addressed a letter of encouragement (257)

Name: St. Agapius of Novara
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Agapius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Novara in Italy (447)

Name: St. Veranus of Vence
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Veranus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Son of St. Eucherius of Lyons, a monk of Lerins who became Bishop of Vence in southern Gaul (c. 480)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Finnian of Movilla
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Finnian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Founder and first abbot of Moville in Ulster, a teacher famed for sanctity and miracles, honored as patron of Ulster (579)

Name: St. Salvius of Albi
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Salvius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A lawyer who became a monk, hermit, and Bishop of Albi, and who died tending the sick during an epidemic (584)

Name: St. Candida the Younger of Naples
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Candida, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A married woman of Naples who sanctified herself in the faithful living of marriage and motherhood (586)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: Hieromartyr Theodard of Maastricht
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Theodard, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Maastricht, murdered by robbers while traveling in defense of his church (c. 670)

Name: St. Autbert of Avranches
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Autbert, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Avranches and founder of the monastery of Mont-Saint-Michel on the Normandy coast (c. 709)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Frithestan of Winchester
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Frithestan, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of St. Grimbald consecrated Bishop of Winchester by St. Plegmund, who loved the poor and prayed much for the departed (933)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: St. Peter of Mozonzo
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Peter, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk and abbot who became Archbishop of Compostela in Spain (c. 1000)
Sources: Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome

Name: Venerable Esaias of Kykkos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Esaias, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Founder of the Kykkos Monastery in Cyprus (12th c.)

Name: Venerable Paul the Obedient
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Paul, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Kiev Caves renowned for his perfect obedience (13th-14th c.)

Name: St. Ioasaph of Kubensk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Ioasaph, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A prince who became a monk and ascetic at Kubensk in Vologda (1453)

Name: St. Cassian of Spaso-Kamenny
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Cassian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Abbot of the Spaso-Kamenny and White Lake monasteries in the Russian north (1469)

Name: St. Theodoritus of Ryazan
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Theodoritus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Archbishop of Ryazan and Murom, a missionary among the peoples of the Volga (1617)

Name: Venerable Tikhon of Kapsala
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Tikhon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Russian-born ascetic of Kapsala on Mount Athos who spent sixty years in unceasing prayer; spiritual father of St. Paisios the Athonite (1968)

Name: New Hieromartyr Meletius Fedyunev
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyr Meletius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A hieromonk of Kuzhba in the Komi land, martyred in the Soviet persecution (1937)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Hieromartyr Gabriel Yatsik
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyr Gabriel, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An archimandrite of the Donskoy Monastery in Moscow, martyred in the Soviet persecution (1937)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: Twelve New Hieromartyr Priests of 1937
Also Known As: Ismael Kudryavtsev, Eugene Popov, John Popov, Constantine Kolpetsky, Peter Grigoriev, Basil Maximov, Gleb Apukhtin, Basil Malinin, John Sofronov, Peter Yurkov, Nicholas Pavlinov, Palladius Popov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Twelve priests martyred together in the Soviet persecution (1937)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Martyr Symeon Turkin
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Martyr Symeon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A layman martyred in the Soviet persecution (1937)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: Virgin-Martyr Tatiana Grimblit
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Martyr Tatiana, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young woman who devoted herself to feeding and aiding prisoners and the persecuted, repeatedly arrested and finally shot in the Soviet persecution (1937)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Hieromartyr Warus of Lipetsk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Hieromartyr Warus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Lipetsk, martyred in the Soviet persecution (1938)
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: New Martyrs of Rmanj
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Martyrs of Rmanj, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Monks and clergy of the Rmanj Monastery who suffered for the faith through the monastery's long history; their relics were uncovered in 2022 and they were numbered among the saints by the Serbian Church

Name: Martyr Basil of Ancyra
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Basil, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest of Ancyra who openly rebuked the revival of paganism under Julian the Apostate and was cruelly tortured and martyred for refusing to renounce Christ.

Name: Righteous Emilia, Mother of St. Basil the Great
Also Known As: St. Emmelia; Emilia of Caesarea; Emmelia of Cappadocia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Emilia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Mother of a household of saints — among them Basil the Great, Gregory of Nyssa, Macrina the Younger, and Peter of Sebaste — she raised her children in piety and ended her life in monastic stillness.
Notes: Commemorated on the Greek calendar on May 30.

Name: Monk-Martyr Telemachus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Martyr Telemachus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk from the East who entered the Colosseum to halt a gladiatorial combat and was killed by the crowd; his death moved the emperor Honorius to abolish the games.

Name: St. Sylvester, Pope of Rome
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Sylvester, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Pope of Rome during the reign of St. Constantine the Great, under whom the Church gained peace after the persecutions; tradition credits him with baptizing the emperor.

Name: Venerable Sylvester of the Kiev Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Sylvester, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Kiev Caves Lavra remembered for obedience and ceaseless prayer.

Name: Righteous Juliana of Lazarevo
Also Known As: Juliana Lazarevskaya; Juliana of Murom; Juliana the Merciful
Life Experience: Widowhood; Loss of a Child; Poverty
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Juliana, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A married noblewoman of Murom who, amid family duties, famine, and the loss of children, gave herself wholly to caring for the poor and sick, selling her possessions to feed the hungry.

Name: Hieromartyr Theogenes, Bishop of Parium
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Theogenes, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Parium on the Hellespont, martyred by drowning for refusing military service and confessing Christ.

Name: New Martyr George of Iberia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr George, pray to God for us.
Notes: Needs clergy/source review (obscure modern martyr).

Name: Holy Prophet Malachi
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Prophet Malachi, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The last of the Old Testament prophets, who foretold the coming of the Forerunner and the Sun of Righteousness.

Name: Martyr Gordius of Caesarea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Gordius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A centurion of Caesarea in Cappadocia who withdrew to the wilderness, then returned to confess Christ openly in the arena and was martyred.

Name: St. Euthymius (Takaishvili) the Man of God
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Euthymius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Georgian historian who guarded his nation's sacred treasures through revolution and exile, living in voluntary poverty; he was called 'the Man of God.'
Notes: Glorified by the Church of Georgia in 2002. Modern saint — clergy/source review advised.

Name: Synaxis of the Seventy Apostles
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostles of the Seventy, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A common commemoration of the seventy disciples whom the Lord sent out two by two to proclaim the Gospel — the first laborers of the apostolic Church.

Name: Venerable Theoctistus of Cucumo in Sicily
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Theoctistus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Abbot of the monastery at Cucumo in Sicily and a guide of monastics.

Name: St. Eustathius I, Archbishop of Serbia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Eustathius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Archbishop of Serbia, a successor in the see of St. Sava, who shepherded the Serbian Church in holiness.

Name: Venerable Aquila of the Kiev Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Aquila, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A deacon of the Kiev Caves Lavra, ascetic in fasting and prayer.

Name: Confessors Zosimus the Hermit and Athanasius
Also Known As: Zosimus the Hermit; Athanasius the Commentarisius (the jailer)
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Confessors Zosimus and Athanasius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A hermit arrested under Diocletian, and the prison official Athanasius who, witnessing his steadfastness, confessed Christ and fled with him to the wilderness.

Name: St. Nikephoros the Leper
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Nikephoros, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of Crete who bore leprosy for decades with serene patience and unceasing prayer, becoming a comfort to fellow sufferers.
Notes: Glorified by the Ecumenical Patriarchate in 2012. Modern saint.

Name: Venerable-Martyr Euthymius and Twelve Monks of Mount Athos
Also Known As: Euthymius and the twelve Athonite monks
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Venerable Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An abbot and twelve monks of Mount Athos martyred for confessing Orthodoxy against pressure for union with Rome.
Notes: Obscure cohort — clergy/source review advised.

Name: Venerable New Martyr Onuphrius of Hilandar
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Onuphrius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of Hilandar on Mount Athos who suffered martyrdom for Christ at Chios.
Notes: Modern martyr — clergy/source review advised.

Name: Hieromartyr Theopemptus and Martyr Theonas
Also Known As: Theopemptus of Nicomedia; Theonas the former magician
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Theopemptus and Martyr Theonas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Theopemptus, bishop of Nicomedia, and the magician Theonas who — converted by the bishop's endurance under torture — was baptized and martyred with him under Diocletian.

Name: Venerable Synkletike of Alexandria
Life Experience: Wealth; Illness; Chronic Pain; Despondency / Acedia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Synkletike, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A wealthy and beautiful Alexandrian who renounced everything for hidden asceticism and became a teacher of the spiritual life; her sayings on despondency endure, and she bore a long, agonizing final illness with thanksgiving.

Name: Holy Prophet Micah
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Prophet Micah, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Old Testament prophet and companion of the Prophet Elias who boldly foretold the defeat of King Ahab and suffered for his witness.
Notes: The son of Imlah (1 Kings 22); distinct from Micah of Moresheth, the writing prophet (Aug 14).

Name: Venerable Apollinaria of Egypt
Also Known As: Apollinaria the Senator's Daughter; the monk Dorotheus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Apollinaria, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Daughter of a Roman official who, fleeing marriage, lived disguised as the monk Dorotheus in great asceticism in the Egyptian desert.

Name: Venerable Phosterius the Hermit
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Phosterius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A hermit who lived in ascetic solitude on a mountain and contended against the iconoclast heresy.

Name: Venerable Menas of Sinai
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Menas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of Mount Sinai who lived in asceticism for more than fifty years.

Name: Venerable Gregory of Crete
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Gregory, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of Crete who endured hardship and persecution during the iconoclast period.

Name: New Martyr Romanus of Karpenisi
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Romanus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of Karpenisi in Greece who was beheaded by the Turks in 1694 for confessing Christ.

Name: Venerable Macarius of Mount Athos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Macarius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Abbot of the Pantocrator Monastery and an imperial chaplain who shone in monastic virtue at the close of the Byzantine age.

Name: Venerable Pakhomios of Keno Lake
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Pakhomios, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of St. Alexander of Oshevensk who founded a hermitage by Lake Keno in the Russian north.

Name: St. Brannock of Braunton
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Brannock, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A missionary monk who settled at Braunton in Devon, remembered for his care of animals and the founding of a church there.
Notes: Local pre-schism Western saint — clergy/source review advised.

Name: St. Cedd of Lastingham
Also Known As: Cedd, Bishop of the East Saxons
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Cedd, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Northumbrian-born bishop who preached the Gospel among the East Saxons and founded the monastery of Lastingham; brother of St. Chad.
Notes: Local pre-schism Western saint — clergy/source review advised.

Name: Venerable George the Chozebite
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father George, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A native of Cyprus who became abbot of the monastery of Choziba in the Judean wilderness, renowned for humility and ascetic labor.

Name: Venerable Domnica of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Domnica, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Came from Carthage to Constantinople and was baptized by Patriarch Nektarios, founding a community of nuns and shining in the gift of wonderworking.

Name: St. Emilian the Confessor, Bishop of Cyzicus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Emilian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Cyzicus who boldly defended the holy icons under the iconoclast emperor Leo the Armenian and died in exile for the faith.

Name: Venerable Gregory, Wonderworker of the Kiev Near Caves
Also Known As: Gregory the Wonderworker of the Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Gregory, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Kiev Caves in the time of St. Theodosius, granted the gift of wonderworking and of overcoming thieves by gentleness.

Name: Venerable Gregory the Recluse of the Kiev Caves
Also Known As: Gregory the Recluse of the Far Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Gregory, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A recluse of the Kiev Caves who lived in strict enclosure in the fourteenth century.

Name: Hieromartyr Isidore and Seventy-Two Martyrs of Yuriev
Also Known As: Isidore of Yuriev and companions; the martyrs of Tartu
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Isidore and companions, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest of Yuriev (Tartu) drowned with seventy-two of his flock for refusing to abandon Orthodoxy.

Name: St. Paisius of Uglich
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Paisius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Igumen of the Protection monastery near Uglich and a builder of monastic life in the Russian north.

Name: Martyr Carterius of Caesarea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Carterius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest of Caesarea in Cappadocia who was tortured and martyred during the persecution of Diocletian.

Name: Martyrs Theophilus the Deacon and Helladius
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Theophilus and Helladius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A deacon and his companion martyred in Libya for confessing Christ.

Name: Martyrs Julian and Basilissa
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Julian and Basilissa, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A married couple of Antinoe in Egypt who by mutual consent lived in chastity, gave their home to the poor and sick, and were martyred under Diocletian.

Name: Venerable Elias the Hermit of Egypt
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Elias, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Egyptian hermit who pursued asceticism for seventy-five years on a desolate mountain.

Name: Martyr Abo of Tbilisi
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Abo, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Arab perfumer who embraced Christ in Georgia and was beheaded by the Saracens at Tbilisi for refusing to return to Islam; he is honored as a patron of the city.
Notes: A convert from Islam; patron of Tbilisi.

Name: Venerable Gregory of Ochrid
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Gregory, pray to God for us.
Notes: Obscure — clergy/source review advised.

Name: Martyr Polyeuktos of Melitene
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Polyeuktos, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Roman soldier of Melitene in Armenia and the first martyr of that city, who smashed the idols and was beheaded for Christ.

Name: Hieromartyr Philip, Metropolitan of Moscow
Also Known As: Philip II of Moscow; Philip Kolychev
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Philip, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Metropolitan of Moscow who openly rebuked the cruelties of Tsar Ivan the Terrible, was deposed and exiled, and was strangled in prison for his witness to justice and truth.

Name: Holy Prophet Shemaiah
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Prophet Shemaiah, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Old Testament prophet who spoke the word of the Lord in the days of King Rehoboam.

Name: St. Peter, Bishop of Sebaste
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Peter, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Youngest brother of Sts. Basil the Great, Gregory of Nyssa, and Macrina, raised by his sister in piety and made bishop of Sebaste.
Notes: Brother of Sts. Basil the Great, Gregory of Nyssa, Macrina, and Theosebia.

Name: St. Eustratius the Wonderworker
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Eustratius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An abbot of the region of Tarsus granted the gift of wonderworking.

Name: St. Gregory of Nyssa
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Gregory, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Younger brother of St. Basil the Great and a profound theologian who, at the Second Ecumenical Council, helped defend the divinity of the Holy Spirit against the Arians.
Notes: Brother of Sts. Basil the Great, Peter of Sebaste, Macrina, and Theosebia.

Name: St. Dometian, Bishop of Melitene
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Dometian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Melitene in the reign of Justin the Younger, a peacemaker entrusted with imperial embassies who cared for the poor.

Name: St. Marcian the Presbyter of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Father Marcian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A presbyter and treasurer of the Great Church in Constantinople who built and adorned many churches and gave generously to the poor.

Name: Venerable Paul of Obnora
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Paul, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of St. Sergius of Radonezh who lived as a hermit in the Vologda forests and founded the monastery of Obnora.

Name: Venerable Makarios of Pisma
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Makarios, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A companion of St. Paul of Obnora who labored in asceticism in the Kostroma region.

Name: Blessed Theosebia the Deaconess
Short Prayer (Intercession): Blessed Theosebia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Sister of Sts. Basil the Great and Gregory of Nyssa, who served the Church as a deaconess and was praised by St. Gregory the Theologian.
Notes: Sister of Sts. Basil, Gregory of Nyssa, Peter of Sebaste, and Macrina.

Name: Venerable Antipas of Valaam
Also Known As: Antipas of Calapodesti; Antipas the Athonite
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Antipas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Moldavian ascetic who labored on Mount Athos and ended his life in stillness and unceasing prayer at Valaam.

Name: St. Theophan the Recluse, Bishop of Tambov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Theophan, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop who withdrew into reclusion to write and translate, becoming one of the great teachers of prayer and the spiritual life; he rendered the Philokalia into Russian.
Notes: Glorified by the Russian Church in 1988.

Name: Venerable Theodosius the Cenobiarch
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Theodosius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Cappadocian who organized cenobitic monasticism in the Palestinian desert, founding a great community with hospices for the poor, the sick, and the dying.

Name: Blessed Michael of Klops
Also Known As: Michael the Fool-for-Christ of Klops, Novgorod
Short Prayer (Intercession): Blessed Michael, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A fool-for-Christ of the Klops monastery near Novgorod, of noble birth, who concealed his gifts of prophecy beneath feigned folly.

Name: St. Theodosius of Antioch
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Theodosius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An ascetic near Antioch who founded a monastery and was renowned for his austere labors.

Name: Venerable Theodosius, Metropolitan of Trebizond
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Theodosius, pray to God for us.
Notes: Obscure — clergy/source review advised.

Name: Martyr Tatiana of Rome
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Tatiana, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A deaconess of Rome who served the poor and was cruelly tortured and beheaded for Christ; she is honored in Russia as a patroness of students and universities.
Notes: Patroness of students; her feast is celebrated as Students' Day in the Russian tradition.

Name: Venerable Martinian of Belozersk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Martinian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of St. Cyril of White Lake who guided the monasteries of Ferapontov and St. Sergius's Lavra.

Name: Martyr Mertius of Mauretania
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Mertius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A soldier martyred under Diocletian for confessing Christ.

Name: Martyr Peter Apselamus of Palestine
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Peter, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young man of Palestine who, refusing every plea to spare himself, was burned alive for confessing Christ.

Name: Venerable Eupraxia of Tabenna
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Eupraxia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A daughter of nobility who renounced wealth and a betrothal to live in deep humility and obedience among the nuns of Tabenna in Egypt.

Name: Virgin Martyrs Neonilla, Domnina and Parthena of Edessa
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Virgin Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Three virgins of Edessa who suffered for Christ.
Notes: In some calendars commemorated on the first Sunday after Theophany.

Name: Martyrs Hermylus and Stratonicus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Hermylus and Stratonicus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A deacon and his jailer-companion, Slavs by origin, drowned in the Danube near Belgrade under the emperor Licinius.

Name: Venerable Irenarchus the Recluse of Rostov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Irenarchus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A recluse of Rostov who bore heavy chains and bonds as ascetic labor and foretold the deliverance of Russia in the Time of Troubles.

Name: Venerable Eleazar of Anzersk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Eleazar, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Founder of the hermitage on Anzer Island at Solovki, a skilled carver who lived in austere solitude.

Name: Martyr Peter of Anium
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Peter, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A confessor slain at Hieropolis for the name of Christ during the early-fourth-century persecutions.

Name: St. James, Bishop of Nisibis
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch James, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Nisibis on the Persian frontier, a wonderworker and one of the fathers of the First Ecumenical Council who protected his city by prayer.

Name: St. Hilary of Poitiers
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Hilary, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Poitiers and a tireless champion of the divinity of Christ against Arianism, exiled for the faith and called the 'Athanasius of the West.'

Name: Venerable Maximus Kavsokalyvites
Also Known As: Maximus the Hut-burner of Mount Athos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Maximus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Athonite ascetic who feigned folly and moved his hut from place to place, granted gifts of prayer and prophecy.

Name: St. Elian of Cornwall
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Father Elian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A missionary who established a church in Cornwall in the fifth century.
Notes: Local pre-schism Western saint — clergy/source review advised.

Name: St. Jeremiah I, Patriarch of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Jeremiah, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Patriarch of Constantinople under Ottoman rule who strengthened the Church and restored its sacred places.

Name: St. Sava I, First Archbishop of Serbia
Also Known As: Sava of Serbia; Rastko Nemanjic; Sabbas
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Sava, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Serbian prince who fled to Mount Athos as a monk, founded Hilandar Monastery with his father St. Symeon, and became the first archbishop and enlightener of the Serbian Church, giving his people law, letters, and spiritual order.
Notes: Patron of Serbia and of Serbian schools.

Name: Holy Fathers Slain at Sinai and Raithu
Also Known As: the Monk-Martyrs of Sinai and Raithu
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Venerable Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The desert ascetics of Mount Sinai and Raithu massacred by raiders, who received the crown of martyrdom in their solitude.

Name: Venerable Joseph Analytinus of Raithu
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Joseph, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An ascetic of Raithu near Sinai who reposed before the slaughter of the desert fathers.

Name: Venerable Theodulus of Sinai
Also Known As: Theodulus, son of St. Nilus of Sinai
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Theodulus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Son of St. Nilus of Sinai, taken captive by raiders and later reunited with his father, who recorded the sufferings of the Sinai fathers.

Name: Venerable Stephen of Khenolakkos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Stephen, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Abbot of the Khenolakkos monastery near Chalcedon.

Name: St. Kentigern of Glasgow
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Kentigern, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: First bishop of Glasgow and apostle of the Britons of Strathclyde, an ascetic missionary affectionately called Mungo.
Notes: Patron of Glasgow; pre-schism Western saint.

Name: St. Meletios, Bishop of Ryazan
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Meletios, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A tireless missionary in Siberia and the Far East who later served as bishop of Ryazan, caring especially for the poor.
Notes: Modern saint — clergy/source review advised.

Name: Hieromartyr Platon of Estonia and the New Martyrs of Estonia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Platon and companions, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The first Orthodox bishop of Estonia, shot by the Bolsheviks in Yuriev (Tartu) in 1919, commemorated with all the New Martyrs of Estonia.
Notes: Glorified 2000 among the New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia.

Name: Venerable Paul of Thebes
Also Known As: Paul the Hermit; Paul the First Hermit
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Paul, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Fleeing the persecution of Decius, he withdrew into the Egyptian desert at a young age and lived there in solitude for some ninety years, honored as the first Christian hermit.

Name: Venerable John Calabytes
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A youth of Constantinople who secretly returned to live as a beggar in a hut by his parents' gate, unrecognized until his death.

Name: Venerable-Martyr Pansophius of Alexandria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Venerable Martyr Pansophius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A desert ascetic who gave his inheritance to the poor and was tortured to death for confessing Christ.

Name: Venerable Prochorus of Pshinja
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Prochorus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A hermit of the Pshinja wilderness who founded a monastery there.

Name: Venerable Gabriel of Lesnovo
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Gabriel, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Founder of the Lesnovo monastery dedicated to the Archangel Michael, around whom many monks gathered.

Name: St. Ita of Killeedy
Also Known As: Ita; Ide; Foster Mother of the Irish Saints
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Ita, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A holy abbess of Killeedy in Ireland who taught and fostered many of the young saints of her land, honored as the foster-mother of the Irish saints.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint.

Name: Holy Women Salome of Ujarma and Perozhavra of Sivnia
Also Known As: Salome of Ujarma; Perozhavra of Sivnia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Righteous Salome and Perozhavra, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Georgian noblewomen who were helpers and close companions of St. Nina in the conversion of Georgia.

Name: Venerable Barlaam of Keretsk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Barlaam, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest of the White Sea coast who, in deep repentance, spent years rowing the northern waters in penance and is venerated as a protector of seafarers and fishermen.

Name: Blessed Maximus of Totma
Short Prayer (Intercession): Blessed Maximus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest of the Vologda region who for forty-five years took up the hard exploit of folly for Christ.

Name: Martyrs Speusippus, Eleusippus, Meleusippus and Leonilla
Also Known As: the triplet martyrs and their grandmother Leonilla
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Triplet brothers and their grandmother Leonilla, who led them to Christ, all martyred under Marcus Aurelius.

Name: Martyr Danax the Reader
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Danax, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A church reader in Macedonia who was slain for refusing to worship idols.

Name: St. Honoratus of Arles
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Honoratus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Founder of the great island monastery of Lérins and later archbishop of Arles, a nursery of bishops and saints in Gaul.

Name: Hieromartyr Damascene the New of Gabrovo
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Damascene, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of Hilandar from Gabrovo, hanged by the Turks for defending his people against a false accusation.
Notes: Modern martyr — clergy/source review advised.

Name: Venerable Romilus of Ravanica
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Romilus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of St. Gregory of Sinai and hesychast who labored in the Balkans and reposed at Ravanica in Serbia.

Name: Venerable Anthony of Dymsk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Anthony, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of St. Barlaam of Khutyn who founded a monastery on Lake Dymsk near Novgorod, where he kept harsh ascetic labors.

Name: Venerable Anthony of Chernoezero
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Anthony, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Founder of a monastery at Black Lake in the Novgorod region.

Name: St. Anthony of Krasny Kholm
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Anthony, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A desert-dweller of the Tver region who founded a monastery by the River Mologa.

Name: Right-believing Emperor Theodosius the Great
Short Prayer (Intercession): Right-believing Emperor Theodosius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The emperor who established the faith of Nicaea throughout the empire and humbly submitted to the Church's penance under St. Ambrose.
Notes: Venerated as a right-believing emperor; included for completeness — clergy/source review advised.

Name: Venerable Achilles the Confessor
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Achilles, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A desert anchorite remembered among the fathers of the Egyptian wilderness.

Name: St. Cyril, Archbishop of Alexandria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Cyril, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Archbishop of Alexandria who presided at the Third Ecumenical Council and defended the Theotokos against Nestorius, confessing the one Christ, God and man.

Name: Venerable Athanasius of Syandemsk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Athanasius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of St. Alexander of Svir who founded a monastery between two lakes in the Vologda land.

Name: Righteous Athanasius of Novolotsk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Athanasius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A righteous man of the Olonets land glorified for his holiness of life.

Name: Venerable Marcian of Cyrrhus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Marcian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A hermit of the Syrian desert near Cyrrhus, renowned for moderation and the gift of healing.

Name: Sts. Cyril and Maria of Radonezh
Also Known As: the parents of St. Sergius of Radonezh
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Righteous Cyril and Maria, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Devout boyars of Rostov, parents of St. Sergius of Radonezh, who raised their children in piety and ended their lives in monastic tonsure.

Name: St. Joachim I, Patriarch of Trnovo
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Joachim, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: First patriarch of Trnovo and a great ascetic who renewed the Bulgarian Church.

Name: St. Maximus Brankovic, Archbishop of Serbia
Also Known As: Maximus the New; Metropolitan of Wallachia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Maximus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A son of the Serbian despot Stephen Brankovic who became a monk, archbishop of Serbia, and metropolitan of Wallachia, a peacemaker among rulers.

Name: St. Ephraim the Lesser of Georgia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Ephraim, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Georgian monk, philosopher, and translator who rendered many patristic works into the Georgian tongue.

Name: Venerable Alexei of Teklati
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Alexei, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Georgian hieromonk who bore illness with patience and shone as a confessor and spiritual guide.
Notes: Modern saint — clergy/source review advised.

Name: Venerable Macarius of Alexandria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Macarius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A friend and contemporary of St. Macarius the Great who spent more than sixty years in extreme asceticism in the Egyptian desert.

Name: St. Mark of Ephesus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Mark, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Archbishop of Ephesus and the lone bishop who refused to sign the false union at the Council of Florence, becoming the great defender of Orthodoxy against compromise.

Name: Venerable Macarius the Faster of the Kiev Caves
Also Known As: Macarius the Deacon of the Near Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Macarius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A deacon of the Kiev Near Caves devoted to fasting and prayer.

Name: Venerable Macarius the Deacon of the Far Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Macarius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A deacon of the Kiev Far Caves distinguished by his freedom from covetousness.

Name: Blessed Theodore of Novgorod, Fool-for-Christ
Short Prayer (Intercession): Blessed Theodore, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A fool-for-Christ of Novgorod who endured cold and mockery, calling the city to repentance.

Name: Venerable Macarius the Roman of Novgorod
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Macarius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A native of Rome who left a wealthy family, embraced Orthodoxy, and lived as a hermit in the swamps near Novgorod.

Name: Virgin-Martyr Euphrasia of Nicomedia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Virgin-Martyr Euphrasia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A noble virgin of Nicomedia who preserved her purity and was beheaded for Christ under Maximian.

Name: St. Arsenius, Archbishop of Kerkyra
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Arsenius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Archbishop of Corfu and a hymnographer who pleaded for his people before the emperor.

Name: St. Anthony of Martqopi
Also Known As: Anthony, Founder of Monasticism in Georgia; one of the 13 Syrian Fathers
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Anthony, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: One of the Thirteen Syrian Fathers who came to Georgia in the sixth century and planted monastic life there, ending his days as a stylite at Martqopi.
Notes: Among the Thirteen Syrian Fathers, whose common commemoration is a movable feast.

Name: Venerable Euthymius the Great
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Euthymius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born to once-childless parents through prayer, he became a father of Palestinian monasticism, founding great lavras and bringing many from heresy to the Orthodox faith.

Name: Venerable Euthymius the Schemamonk of the Kiev Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Euthymius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A schemamonk of the Kiev Caves who kept silence and unceasing prayer.

Name: Venerable Laurence the Recluse of the Kiev Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Laurence, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A recluse of the Kiev Caves, later bishop of Turov, granted gifts of healing.

Name: Venerable Euthymius of Syanzhemsk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Euthymius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An ascetic of the Vologda region who founded a monastery on the River Syanzhema.

Name: Martyrs Inna, Pinna and Rimma
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Disciples of the Apostle Andrew who preached among the Scythians of the lower Danube and were frozen to death in the ice for Christ.

Name: Martyrs Bassus, Eusebius, Eutychius and Basilides
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Noble citizens of Nicomedia martyred under Diocletian for confessing Christ.

Name: St. Euthymius, Patriarch of Trnovo
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Euthymius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The last patriarch of Trnovo, a hesychast and reformer of Slavonic letters who defended Bulgaria's faithful when the Turks took the city.

Name: New Martyr Zachariah of Patras
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Zachariah, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A merchant of the Peloponnese who confessed Christ before the Turks and was martyred.
Notes: Modern martyr — clergy/source review advised.

Name: St. Euthymius the Confessor of Georgia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Euthymius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Georgian monk who labored to preserve and notate the ancient Georgian church chant under Soviet persecution.
Notes: Modern saint — clergy/source review advised.

Name: Venerable Maximus the Confessor
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Maximus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A theologian and former imperial secretary who defended the two wills of Christ against the Monothelites; his tongue and right hand were cut off, yet his confession became the faith of the Sixth Ecumenical Council.

Name: Martyr Neophytus of Nicaea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Neophytus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A youth of Nicaea raised in piety who confessed Christ and was martyred while still a boy.

Name: Martyrs Eugene, Candidus, Valerian and Aquila
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Four confessors of Trebizond who fled to the mountains and were seized and martyred under Diocletian.

Name: Virgin-Martyr Agnes of Rome
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Virgin-Martyr Agnes, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A girl of Rome, barely thirteen, who refused every suitor for the love of Christ and was put to death, becoming one of the most beloved virgin-martyrs of the early Church.

Name: Martyr Anastasius, Disciple of St. Maximus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Confessor Anastasius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of St. Maximus the Confessor who shared his master's sufferings and exile for the true faith.

Name: Venerable Neophytos of Vatopaidi
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Neophytos, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A sacristan of Vatopaidi Monastery on Mount Athos who was granted a vision of the Mother of God.

Name: Venerable Maximus the Greek
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Maximus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A learned monk of Athos invited to Russia to translate the sacred books, who was unjustly imprisoned for many years yet bore it with patience and left a great body of spiritual writing.

Name: Hieromartyr Fructuosus and Deacons Augurius and Eulogius
Also Known As: Fructuosus, Bishop of Tarragona, and his deacons
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Fructuosus and companions, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Tarragona in Spain and his two deacons, burned alive for Christ under Valerian.

Name: St. George (John) of Georgia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father George, pray to God for us.
Notes: Modern Georgian saint — clergy/source review advised.

Name: Apostle Timothy of the Seventy
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostle Timothy, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple and beloved fellow-laborer of the Apostle Paul, who made him first bishop of Ephesus; he is invoked for ailments of the stomach, which St. Paul counseled him to tend.

Name: Venerable-Martyr Anastasius the Persian
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Venerable Martyr Anastasius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Persian soldier, son of a magus, who was drawn to Christ by the holy Cross, became a monk in Jerusalem, and was strangled for the faith under Chosroes II.

Name: Venerable-Martyr Anastasius the Deacon of the Kiev Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Venerable Martyr Anastasius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A deacon of the Kiev Near Caves who shone in monastic obedience.

Name: Venerable Macarius of Zhabyn
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Macarius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A restorer of the Zhabyn monastery near the River Oka who sheltered the poor and even his enemies during the Time of Troubles.

Name: Martyrs Manuel, George, Peter, Leontius and Companions
Also Known As: the 377 Martyrs of Adrianople; the martyrs under the Bulgars
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishops, clergy, and faithful taken captive in Thrace and put to death for refusing to deny Christ during the wars with the pagan Bulgars.

Name: St. Brihtwald of Wilton
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Brihtwald, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of Glastonbury who became bishop of Ramsbury in Wiltshire and was granted gifts of prophecy.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint.

Name: Hieromartyr Clement of Ancyra and Martyr Agathangelus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Clement and Martyr Agathangelus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Ancyra who endured some twenty-eight years of imprisonment and torture, and the young Agathangelus who joined him in confession and martyrdom.

Name: Venerable Gennadius of Kostroma
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Gennadius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of St. Alexander of Svir who founded a monastery in the Kostroma forests.

Name: Venerable Mausimas the Syrian
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Mausimas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A simple-hearted Syrian ascetic near Cyrrhus whose vessels of oil and grain never ran dry as he fed the poor.

Name: Venerable Salamanes the Silent
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Salamanes, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A hermit of the Euphrates who kept perfect silence and stillness, immovable in his enclosure.

Name: St. Paulinus the Merciful of Nola
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Paulinus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A wealthy nobleman and poet of Bordeaux who gave away his riches to the poor, even offering himself in place of a widow's captive son, and became the beloved bishop of Nola.

Name: Venerable Xenia of Rome
Also Known As: Eusebia; Xenia of Rome and her two servants
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Xenia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Roman senator's daughter who fled an arranged marriage with two servants, took the name Xenia ('stranger'), and founded a convent of great humility in Mylasa of Caria.

Name: St. Gerasimus, Bishop of Perm
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Gerasimus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Third bishop of the newly-enlightened Zyrian people, who labored to convert and shepherd them and met a martyr's end.
Notes: Jan 29 is the Synaxis of the hierarchs of Perm.

Name: Martyr John of Kazan
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Russian held captive in Kazan who, refusing to deny Christ, was struck down and left for dead, surviving long enough to receive the Mysteries before his repose.

Name: Martyr Babylas of Sicily and Disciples Timothy and Agapius
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Babylas, Timothy and Agapius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A teacher of Sicily and his two disciples who left the world for the ascetic life and were martyred for Christ.

Name: Venerable Macedonius the Hermit of Syria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Macedonius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Syrian hermit who lived in pits and ravines for some forty-five years, eating only barley.

Name: Venerable Dionysius of Olympus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Dionysius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Athonite and founder of a monastery on Mount Olympus in Greece, a peacemaker and guide of souls.

Name: Venerable Philotheus of Athos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Philotheus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Founder of the Philotheou Monastery on Mount Athos toward the end of the tenth century.

Name: Martyrs Theodotion, Paul and Pausirios
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Confessors of Egypt who suffered for Christ in the third century.

Name: St. Philo, Bishop of Kolpasteia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Philo, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop in Cyprus, ordained by St. Epiphanius, who shepherded his flock and reposed in peace.

Name: Hieromartyr Philippicus and Martyr Barsimos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest and his companion beheaded for confessing Christ.

Name: St. Moses, Archbishop of Novgorod
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Moses, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Archbishop of Novgorod who built many churches and monasteries and cared for his city in difficult times.

Name: Martyr Felicitas of Rome and Her Seven Sons
Also Known As: Felicity of Rome; Januarius, Felix, Philip, Silvanus, Alexander, Vitalius, Martial
Life Experience: Loss of a Child; Widowhood; Persecution
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Felicitas and your sons, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A widow of Rome who, with steadfast faith, encouraged each of her seven sons to die for Christ before her own martyrdom.

Name: St. Publius the Ascetic of Syria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Publius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A senator's son near Zeugma in Syria who sold his goods and gathered a community of Greek and Syrian monks.

Name: St. Mares the Singer of Syria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Mares, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An ascetic of the Cyrrhus region celebrated for the sweetness of his psalmody.

Name: Hieromartyr Vladimir, Metropolitan of Kiev
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Vladimir, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Metropolitan of Kiev and the first bishop-martyr of the Russian Church under the Bolsheviks, shot outside the Kiev Caves Lavra in 1918.
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia. Glorified 1992.

Name: New Martyr Auxentius of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Auxentius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young man of Constantinople who, falsely accused of having embraced Islam, confessed Christ before the Turks and was beheaded.
Notes: Modern martyr — clergy/source review advised.

Name: Venerable Anatole I of Optina
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Anatole, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An elder of Optina, disciple of St. Ambrose, who consoled the many who came to him for spiritual counsel and healing of soul.
Notes: Among the Synaxis of the Optina Elders.

Name: St. Dositheus of Tbilisi
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Dositheus, pray to God for us.
Notes: Georgian hierarch — clergy/source review advised.

Name: St. Gabriel, Bishop of Imereti
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Gabriel, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A learned and beloved Georgian bishop of Imereti, a preacher and benefactor of the poor.
Notes: Modern Georgian saint — clergy/source review advised.

Name: St. Vetranion, Bishop of Tomis
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Vetranion, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Tomis in Scythia Minor who boldly withstood the Arian emperor Valens to his face in defense of the Orthodox faith.

Name: Venerable Xenophon, his Wife Mary, and their Sons Arcadius and John
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Righteous Xenophon, Mary, Arcadius and John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A devout family of Constantinople who, after their sons were thought lost in a shipwreck, were reunited and all embraced the monastic life, ending their days in holiness.

Name: St. Xenophon of Robeika
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Xenophon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of St. Barlaam of Khutyn and superior of his monastery, founder of a community on the River Robeika.

Name: Martyrs Ananias the Presbyter, Peter, and Seven Soldiers
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest of Phoenicia, a jailer, and seven soldiers converted by his steadfastness, all martyred for Christ.

Name: St. Simeon the Ancient of Mount Sinai
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Simeon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An elder of the Syrian and Sinai wilderness, a guide of monastics and worker of wonders.

Name: St. Joseph, Bishop of Thessalonica
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Joseph, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Brother of St. Theodore the Studite, a hymnographer and bishop of Thessalonica who suffered exile for venerating the holy icons.

Name: Blessed David IV the Builder, King of Georgia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Right-believing King David, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The king who delivered Georgia from invaders and led it into a golden age, building churches and monasteries and ruling with justice and piety.

Name: St. Theodore of Ajareli
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Theodore, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Georgian priest who misled invaders to save his land and was martyred for it.
Notes: Georgian saint — clergy/source review advised.

Name: St. Paula of Rome
Life Experience: Widowhood; Loss of a Child; Wealth; Grief / Bereavement
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Mother Paula, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A noble Roman widow who, after the death of her husband and a daughter, gave her wealth to the poor, followed St. Jerome to the Holy Land, and founded monasteries in Bethlehem.

Name: St. Clement the Stylite
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Clement, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An ascetic of Mount Sagmata in Boeotia who took up the labor of the pillar.

Name: Holy Empress Markiane
Also Known As: Marciana, wife of Emperor Justin I
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Empress Markiane, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The pious wife of the emperor Justin the Elder, known for her ascetic life and love for the poor.
Notes: Clergy/source review advised.

Name: Holy King Ashot of Georgia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Right-believing King Ashot, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Georgian king who built churches and monasteries and was slain by the Arabs at the altar of his castle church.
Notes: Georgian ruler-saint — clergy/source review advised.

Name: Venerable Ephraim of Novy Torg
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Ephraim, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A servant of the holy passion-bearers Boris and Gleb who, after his brother's martyrdom, founded a monastery and hostel at Torzhok.

Name: St. Ephraim, Bishop of Pereyaslavl
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Ephraim, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Kiev Caves and bishop of Pereyaslavl who built hospitals and bathhouses for the care of the sick and poor.

Name: Venerable Palladius the Hermit of Antioch
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Palladius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A hermit near Antioch granted the gift of wonderworking.

Name: Holy Martyrs Archil II and Luarsab of Georgia
Also Known As: Archil and Luarsab, kings of Georgia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Archil and Luarsab, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Georgian kings who confessed Christ before Muslim conquerors and were put to death rather than deny their faith.
Notes: Georgian ruler-martyrs — clergy/source review advised.

Name: Sts. Pitirim and Jonah, Bishops of Perm
Also Known As: Pitirim of Perm; Jonah of Perm
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarchs Pitirim and Jonah, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Successors of St. Stephen and St. Gerasimus in the see of Perm; Pitirim was slain by the pagan Voguls, and Jonah completed the conversion of the Permian peoples.

Name: St. Ignatius, Bishop of Smolensk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Ignatius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of Smolensk venerated as a wonderworker.

Name: Martyrs Romanus, Jacob and Companions of Samosata
Also Known As: Romanus, Jacob, Philotheus, Hyperechius, Abibus, Julian and Paregonius
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Seven citizens of Samosata seized while keeping the feast of the Cross and martyred together for refusing to sacrifice to idols.

Name: Venerable Aphraates the Persian
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Aphraates, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Persian who forsook idolatry for Christ and lived as an ascetic near Antioch, boldly withstanding the Arian emperor Valens.

Name: Hieromartyr Hippolytus of Rome and Companions
Also Known As: Hippolytus, Censorinus, Sabinus, Chryse the Virgin and those with them
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Hippolytus and companions, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A pastor of the Roman Church and a company of martyrs, among them the virgin Chryse, who suffered together for confessing Christ.

Name: Venerable Zeno the Faster of the Kiev Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Zeno, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Kiev Far Caves, a lover of fasting and of labor.

Name: Venerable Zeno the Hermit of Antioch
Also Known As: Zeno, disciple of St. Basil the Great
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Zeno, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A courier of the imperial post who, moved by St. Basil the Great, gave away his wealth and lived in a tomb near Antioch in great austerity.

Name: Martyr Theophilus the New
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Theophilus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Byzantine commander taken captive by the Arabs who, repenting of a moment's weakness, confessed Christ and was beheaded rather than deny the faith.

Name: Blessed Peter, King of Bulgaria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Right-believing King Peter, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Son of Tsar Simeon, a peaceful and pious king of Bulgaria who befriended St. John of Rila and cared for the Church and the poor.

Name: New Martyr Demetrius of Sliven
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Demetrius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young man of Sliven in Bulgaria martyred by the Turks for confessing Christ.
Notes: Modern martyr — clergy/source review advised.

Name: Holy Unmercenaries Cyrus and John
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Unmercenaries Cyrus and John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A physician of Alexandria who healed soul and body without payment, and a soldier who joined him; both were martyred under Diocletian and are invoked for healing of the sick.

Name: Venerable Niketas the Recluse, Bishop of Novgorod
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Niketas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A recluse of the Kiev Caves who, after being deceived in his enclosure by the enemy, was restored through the prayers of the fathers and later became a holy bishop of Novgorod.

Name: Martyrs Victorinus, Victor and Companions of Corinth
Also Known As: Victorinus, Victor, Nikephoros, Claudius, Diodorus, Serapion and Papias
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A company of confessors who suffered terrible torments at Corinth under the persecution of Decius.

Name: Martyr Tryphaena of Cyzicus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Tryphaena, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A senator's daughter of Cyzicus who offered herself for Christ and was martyred.

Name: St. Arsenius of Paros
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Arsenius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A teacher and spiritual father on the island of Paros, an ascetic and wonderworker who guided many souls.
Notes: Glorified 1967.

Name: St. Julius of Aegina
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Father Julius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A native of Aegina who studied in Athens alongside Sts. Basil and Gregory and built many churches.

Name: Martyrs Perpetua, Felicitas, and Companions
Also Known As: Perpetua; Felicity; Saturus; Revocatus; Saturninus; Secundulus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Perpetua, Felicitas, and your companions, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A group of catechumens martyred at Carthage in 203 under Septimius Severus; Perpetua, a young noblewoman, and Felicitas, a slave who gave birth in prison, went to the wild beasts together.
Notes: Among the most celebrated of the early African martyrs.

Name: Venerable Peter the Hermit of Galatia
Also Known As: Peter of Galatia; Peter the Ascetic
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Peter, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An ascetic from Galatia who settled in a tomb near Antioch in Syria, living in extreme simplicity and granted the gift of healing; he reposed at a great age.

Name: Venerable Vendemianus the Hermit of Bithynia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Vendemianus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of Saint Auxentius who lived as a hermit for many years on Mount Oxia in Bithynia, struggling in solitude and prayer.

Name: Saint Tryphon, Bishop of Rostov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Tryphon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Abbot of the Novospassky Monastery in Moscow who was consecrated Bishop of Rostov in 1462 and shepherded his see in piety.
Notes: Distinct from the Martyr Tryphon of Lampsacus, also commemorated this day.

Name: Saint Seiriol of Wales
Also Known As: Seiriol the Fair; Seiriol of Anglesey
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Seiriol, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A sixth-century Welsh monk who founded a monastery on Anglesey and lived as a hermit on the island that bears his name.

Name: Righteous Simeon the God-Receiver
Also Known As: Simeon the God-bearer; Simeon who received the Lord
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy and Righteous Simeon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The God-Receiver who, as the Holy Spirit had promised, lived to take the infant Christ into his arms in the Temple, proclaiming "Now lettest Thou Thy servant depart in peace"; tradition counts him among the seventy translators of the Septuagint.

Name: Righteous Anna the Prophetess
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy and Righteous Anna, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An aged widow and prophetess who served God with fasting and prayer night and day in the Temple, and who recognized and proclaimed the infant Christ at His Meeting.
