Name: Right-believing Prince Roman of Uglich
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Right-believing Prince Roman, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Prince of Uglich (born 1235) who, after the death of his wife, devoted himself to building churches, caring for the poor, and visiting the sick.

Name: Prophet Azariah
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Prophet Azariah, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Old Testament prophet who in the reign of King Asa of Judah called the people to turn from idols and seek the Lord (2 Chronicles 15).

Name: Martyrs Papias, Diodorus, and Claudianus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Papias, Diodorus, and Claudianus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Three Christians martyred together at Perge in Pamphylia about the year 250 during the persecution of Decius.

Name: Saint Savva of Ioannina
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Savva, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An ascetic and spiritual father of the fifteenth century who labored in a monastery on an island in the lake of Ioannina in Epiros.

Name: Martyr Adrian of Caesarea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Adrian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: One of the last martyrs of the persecution at Caesarea in Palestine under Galerius; he was condemned and slain by the sword about the year 309.

Name: Martyr Eubulus of Caesarea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Eubulus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A companion of Saint Adrian and the very last to suffer in the persecution at Caesarea in Palestine, given to the beasts and then slain about the year 309.
Notes: Commemorated together with the Martyr Adrian.

Name: Martyr Blaise the Shepherd
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Blaise, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A shepherd of Caesarea in Cappadocia who was seized for his faith, tortured, and beheaded during the persecution of the Christians.
Notes: Distinct from Saint Blaise, Bishop of Sebaste (Feb 11).

Name: Saint Simeon, Bishop of Polotsk and Tver
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Simeon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A descendant of the princes of Polotsk who became the first Bishop of Tver, renowned for boldly admonishing princes to rule with justice.

Name: Saint Jacob, Archbishop of Serbia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Jacob, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Archbishop of Serbia in the late thirteenth century, a successor of Saint Sava who governed the Serbian Church in piety and care for the poor.

Name: St. Nicholas, Enlightener of Japan
Also Known As: Nicholas of Japan; Nikolai Kasatkin; Nicholas Kasatkin
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Nicholas, Equal-to-the-Apostles, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Russian priest-monk who in 1861 began missionary work in Japan, founding the Orthodox Church there, translating the Scriptures and services into Japanese, and serving as its first archbishop; he reposed in 1912.
Notes: Glorified in 1970.

Name: Venerable Isidore of Pelusium
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Isidore, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A learned monk and priest near Pelusium in Egypt, an admirer of Saint John Chrysostom, who left some two thousand letters of spiritual and scriptural counsel.

Name: Right-believing Great Prince George of Vladimir
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Right-believing Prince George, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Grand Prince of Vladimir (born 1189) who founded Nizhny Novgorod and fell in battle against the Mongol invaders at the river Sit in 1238.

Name: Venerable Cyril, Abbot of Novoezersk
Also Known As: Cyril of Novoezersk; Cyril of New Lake
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Cyril, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of Saint Cornelius of Komel who founded a monastery on an island of New Lake (Novoozero) in the Novgorod land and was glorified by wonders; he reposed in 1532.

Name: Venerable Abraham and Coprius of Pechenga
Also Known As: Abraham of Pechenga; Coprius of Pechenga
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Fathers Abraham and Coprius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two monks who in 1492 founded the Savior wilderness monastery on the river Pechenga in the Vologda land, laboring together in asceticism.

Name: Martyr Jadorus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Jadorus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A martyr who suffered with Saint Isidore in the reign of the emperor Decius (249-251).

Name: Hieromartyr Abramius, Bishop of Arbela
Also Known As: Abraham of Arbela; Abramius of Arbela in Assyria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Abramius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Arbela in Assyria who was seized and martyred for refusing to worship the sun during the persecution of the Persian king Sapor (Shapur) II.

Name: Venerable Nicholas the Confessor, Abbot of Studion
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Nicholas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Cretan who entered the Studion monastery in Constantinople and suffered imprisonment and exile for venerating the holy icons; he later became abbot and reposed about 868.
Notes: A confessor in the defense of the holy icons.

Name: Saint Evagrius of Shiomghvime
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Evagrius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Georgian nobleman who, drawn by the example of Saint Shio of Mgvime, abandoned the world to become his disciple and a monk in the wilderness.

Name: Monastic Martyr Anthony of Karyes
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Martyr Anthony, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A man of the Ukrainian land, baptized Onouphrios, who in deep repentance for a killing embraced the monastic life at Karyes on Mount Athos and ended his days as a martyr before 1506.

Name: Martyr Agatha of Palermo
Also Known As: Agatha of Sicily; Agatha of Catania
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Agatha, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young noblewoman of Sicily who consecrated her virginity to Christ and, refusing the advances of the governor Quintianus, endured cruel torture and martyrdom about the year 251 under Decius.

Name: Martyr Theodula and her Companions
Also Known As: Theodula of Anazarbus; Helladius; Macarius; Evagrius
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Theodula, Helladius, Macarius, and Evagrius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Theodula, a Christian woman of Anazarbus in Cilicia, was martyred under Diocletian together with Helladius, Macarius, and Evagrius, who were converted by the sight of her steadfastness.

Name: Saint Bucolus, Bishop of Smyrna
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Bucolus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of Saint John the Theologian who was appointed first Bishop of Smyrna, converted many pagans, and consecrated Saint Polycarp as his successor.

Name: St. Photius the Great, Patriarch of Constantinople
Also Known As: Photios the Great; Photius of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Photius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The great ninth-century Patriarch of Constantinople, a man of vast learning, defender of Orthodoxy, and a sender-forth of the mission to the Slavs; he twice suffered deposition and exile.

Name: Venerable Barsanuphius the Great and John the Prophet
Also Known As: Barsanuphius of Gaza; John the Prophet of Gaza
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Fathers Barsanuphius and John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two recluses of a monastery near Gaza in the sixth century whose written answers to questions of monks and laymen form a treasured guide of spiritual discernment.

Name: Virgin Martyr Dorothy of Caesarea
Also Known As: Dorothy of Caesarea in Cappadocia; Christina; Callista; Theophilus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Dorothy and your companions, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian virgin of Caesarea in Cappadocia martyred under Diocletian; the lawyer Theophilus, who mocked her, was converted by a miracle and martyred with the sisters Christina and Callista.

Name: Martyr Julian of Emesa
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Julian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A physician of Emesa in Syria who secretly tended and encouraged imprisoned confessors, and who was himself seized and martyred about the year 312.

Name: Virgin Martyr Fausta and her Companions
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Fausta, Evilasius, and Maximus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young Christian maiden of Cyzicus whose constancy under torture converted her persecutors, the elder Evilasius and the eparch Maximus, who suffered martyrdom with her.

Name: Martyrs Martha, Mary, and Lycarion
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Martha, Mary, and Lycarion, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two sisters of Egypt who openly confessed Christ and, with their young brother Lycarion, were put to death for their faith.

Name: Saint Arsenius of Iqalto
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Arsenius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A learned Georgian monk, translator and hymnographer who founded the academy of Iqalto and defended the Orthodox faith in his writings.

Name: Saint Amand of Maastricht
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Amand, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of Aquitaine who became a tireless missionary bishop, preaching the Gospel through Flanders and the Low Countries and founding many monasteries; he reposed about 679.

Name: Saint Parthenios, Bishop of Lampsacus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Parthenios, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The son of a deacon of Melitopolis who, though unlettered, learned the Scriptures by heart; consecrated Bishop of Lampsacus in the time of Saint Constantine, he was renowned for casting out demons and healing the sick.

Name: Venerable Luke of Hellas
Also Known As: Luke of Steiris; Hosios Loukas; Luke the Younger
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Luke, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Greek of peasant family who from youth gave to the poor and embraced the ascetic life at Steiris in Phocis, where he was granted the gift of wonders; the great monastery of Hosios Loukas bears his name.

Name: The 1,003 Martyrs of Nicomedia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs of Nicomedia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: One thousand and three men, women, and children, the households of the dignitaries Bassos, Eusebius, Eutychius, and Basilides, who confessed Christ and were cut down by soldiers at Nicomedia in 303 under Diocletian.

Name: Martyr Aule of London
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Aule, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A martyr who suffered for Christ at London during the persecution of Diocletian.
Notes: An obscure entry of the British martyrology; details are scant.

Name: Greatmartyr Theodore Stratelates
Also Known As: Theodore the General; Theodore Stratelates of Heraclea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Greatmartyr Theodore, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A military commander (stratelates) of Heraclea who confessed Christ before the emperor Licinius, destroyed the city's idols, and was tortured and beheaded for the faith. Feb 8 commemorates his martyrdom; June 8 the translation of his relics.
Notes: Principal commemoration of his repose; the translation of his relics is kept June 8.

Name: Prophet Zachariah the Sickle-Seer
Also Known As: Zechariah; Zacharias; eleventh of the Twelve Minor Prophets
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Prophet Zachariah, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: One of the Twelve Minor Prophets, who returned from the Babylonian captivity and prophesied the rebuilding of the Temple and the coming of the Messiah; called the 'sickle-seer' from the vision of the flying scroll.
Notes: Not to be confused with the Prophet Zachariah, father of St. John the Forerunner.

Name: Saint Sava II, Archbishop of Serbia
Also Known As: Predislav Nemanjic; Sabbas II of Serbia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Sava, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Son of King Stefan the First-Crowned and a nephew of St. Sava I, who became a monk on Mount Athos and later Archbishop of Serbia, shepherding the Serbian Church in the thirteenth century.

Name: Blessed Liubov of Ryazan, Fool-for-Christ
Short Prayer (Intercession): Blessed Liubov, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bedridden young woman of Ryazan who, after a miraculous healing, took up the podvig of folly for Christ, becoming known for clairvoyance and consolation; she reposed in 1921 and was glorified in 1998.

Name: Martyr Nikephoros of Antioch
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Nikephoros, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A layman of Antioch who, reconciling with the priest Sapricius after a bitter quarrel, received the crown of martyrdom in his place when Sapricius, refusing to forgive, denied Christ at the last moment; martyred under Valerian.
Notes: His life is a celebrated lesson on the necessity of forgiveness and love.

Name: Venerable Pancratius of the Kiev Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Pancratius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A hieromonk and recluse of the Kiev Caves who was granted the gift of healing the sick through prayer and fasting; his relics rest in the Far Caves.

Name: Venerable Nikephoros of Vazheozersk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Nikephoros, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of St. Alexander of Svir who, with St. Gennadius, founded a hermitage by Lake Vazhe in the northern Russian wilderness.

Name: Venerable Gennadius of Vazheozersk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Gennadius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of St. Alexander of Svir who labored in solitude by Lake Vazhe and, with St. Nikephoros, established the Vazheozersk hermitage; reposed in 1516.

Name: Hieromartyrs Marcellus of Sicily, Philagrius of Cyprus, and Pancratius of Taormina
Also Known As: Marcellus, Bishop of Sicily; Philagrius, Bishop of Cyprus; Pancratius, Bishop of Taormina
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyrs Marcellus, Philagrius, and Pancratius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Three bishops counted among the disciples of the Apostle Peter, sent to evangelize Sicily and Cyprus, who suffered for preaching the Gospel.
Notes: One commemoration of three named bishop-martyrs, disciples of the Apostle Peter.

Name: Venerable Shio of Mgvime
Also Known As: Shio Mgvime; Shio of the Cave
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Shio, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: One of the Thirteen Syrian (Assyrian) Fathers who brought monasticism to Georgia; he withdrew to a cave near Mtskheta and founded the great Shio-Mgvime Monastery.
Notes: Also commemorated May 9. One of the Thirteen Syrian Fathers of Georgia.

Name: Virgin-Martyr Apollonia of Alexandria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Virgin-Martyr Apollonia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An aged virgin of Alexandria seized in the persecution under Decius; her teeth were broken out before she went willingly into the fire rather than deny Christ.
Notes: Traditionally invoked against toothache and dental suffering; this is a pastoral, not a medical, facet.

Name: Venerable Romanos of Cilicia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Romanos, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A hermit of Cilicia who dwelt in a cave near Antioch in great austerity and was granted the gift of healing and of obtaining children for the childless through his prayers.
Notes: Also commemorated November 27.

Name: Martyrs Porphyrius and Baptus, with Three Women Martyrs
Also Known As: Porphyrius; Baptus; the Three Women Martyrs of Magnesia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Porphyrius, Baptus, and your companions, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two soldiers and three women who, witnessing the steadfastness of the Hieromartyr Haralambos under torture, confessed Christ and were martyred with him at Magnesia.
Notes: Companions of the Hieromartyr Charalampos (Haralambos), commemorated the same day.

Name: Synaxis of the Hierarchs of Novgorod
Also Known As: Joachim of Korsun; Luke the Jew; Germanus; Arcadius; Gregory; Martyrius; Anthony; Basil the Lame; Simeon; Gennadius; Pimen; Aphthonius
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarchs of Novgorod, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A common commemoration of the bishops and archbishops who shepherded the see of Novgorod from St. Joachim of Korsun (10th-11th c.) onward, many of them wonderworkers whose relics rest in the Cathedral of Holy Wisdom.
Notes: Collective synaxis; also kept Oct 4 and the third Sunday after Pentecost. Era/century reflect the founding bishop; the synaxis spans the 11th-17th centuries.

Name: Princess Anna of Novgorod
Also Known As: Anna of Novgorod; Ingegerd of Sweden; Irene
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Righteous Anna, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Swedish princess (Ingegerd) who married Grand Prince Yaroslav the Wise of Kiev, took the name Anna, and ended her life as a nun in Novgorod; mother of many princes and one of the first canonized women of Rus'.

Name: Venerable Prochorus of the Kiev Near Caves
Also Known As: Prochorus the Wonderworker; Prochorus the Goosefoot
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Prochorus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Kiev Caves who, during a famine, miraculously made sweet bread from bitter goosefoot weed and salt from ashes, feeding the hungry; called the 'Goosefoot' for this wonder. Reposed 1107.

Name: Venerable Longinus of Koryazhemsk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Longinus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk who founded the Koryazhemsk Monastery in the Vologda wilderness, laboring in austerity; reposed in 1540.

Name: Virgin-Martyrs Ennatha, Valentina, and Paula of Palestine
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Virgin-Martyrs Ennatha, Valentina, and Paula, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Three Christian virgins of Palestine who suffered scourging, fire, and beheading at Caesarea during the persecution of Maximinus in 308.
Notes: One commemoration of three named virgin-martyrs.

Name: Saint John Chimchimeli the Philosopher
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A learned Georgian monk, theologian, and translator known as 'the Philosopher' for his erudition and commentaries.

Name: Hieromartyr Blaise, Bishop of Sebaste
Commonly Asked Intercessions: Healing; Animals / Livestock; Protection from Illness / Epidemic
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Blaise, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Sebaste in Armenia who withdrew to a cave during persecution, where wild beasts came to him for blessing; arrested under Licinius, he healed a boy choking on a fishbone before his martyrdom. Invoked for ailments of the throat and for the protection of livestock.
Notes: Distinct from the Martyr Blaise the Shepherd of Caesarea in Cappadocia.

Name: Venerable Demetrius of Priluki, Vologda
Also Known As: Demetrios of Priluki; Dmitry of Priluki
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Demetrius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A friend and disciple of St. Sergius of Radonezh who founded the Savior-Priluki Monastery near Vologda and was renowned for his love of the poor; reposed 1392.

Name: Saint Vsevolod-Gabriel, Wonderworker of Pskov
Also Known As: Vsevolod of Pskov; Gabriel in Baptism; Vsevolod-Gabriel of Novgorod
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Right-believing Prince Vsevolod, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Grandson of Vladimir Monomakh, prince of Novgorod and later Pskov, who built churches, cared for the poor, and was beloved by the people of Pskov; reposed 1138 and glorified as a wonderworker.

Name: Righteous Theodora the Empress, Restorer of the Icons
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Righteous Theodora, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Wife of the iconoclast emperor Theophilus who, after his death, convened the council that restored the veneration of the holy icons in 843, the event celebrated as the Triumph of Orthodoxy.
Notes: Her restoration of the icons is commemorated on the first Sunday of Great Lent (Triumph of Orthodoxy).

Name: New Martyr George of Sofia
Also Known As: George the New of Sofia; George of Kratovo
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr George, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young goldsmith of Sofia under Ottoman rule who refused to convert to Islam and was burned alive for confessing Christ in 1515.

Name: Saint Gobnata of Ballyvourney
Commonly Asked Intercessions: Protection from Illness / Epidemic; Animals / Livestock
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Gobnata, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Irish abbess who founded a monastic community at Ballyvourney in County Cork, famed for her beekeeping and for protecting her people from plague.
Notes: Traditionally invoked against plague; associated with bees.

Name: Saint Meletius, Archbishop of Antioch
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Meletius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Archbishop of Antioch and a champion of Nicene Orthodoxy who endured repeated exiles under Arian emperors; he ordained St. John Chrysostom and presided at the Second Ecumenical Council, where he reposed in 381.

Name: Saint Alexis, Metropolitan of Moscow
Also Known As: Alexei of Moscow; Alexius, Wonderworker of All Russia; Eleutherius Byakont
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Alexis, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Metropolitan of Moscow and regent of the Russian principality during the minority of Demetrius Donskoy, a wonderworker who healed the blind Tatar khansha Taidula; a builder of monasteries and a peacemaker among the princes. Reposed 1378.

Name: Saint Meletius, Archbishop of Kharkov
Also Known As: Meletius Leontovich; Meletius of Kharkov and Akhtyrsk; the Unmercenary Bishop
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Meletius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A nineteenth-century archbishop of Kharkov and Akhtyrsk, called "the Unmercenary Bishop" for giving all he had to the poor; a man of unceasing prayer and night vigils, he reposed in 1840.
Notes: His repose fell on Feb 29; in non-leap years the leap-day commemoration transfers to Feb 28.

Name: Venerable Mary (Marinus) and her father Eugene of Alexandria
Also Known As: Mary called Marinus; Marina the Monk; Eugene her father
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Mary and Father Eugene, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young woman who entered a men's monastery disguised as the monk Marinus to remain with her widowed father Eugene; falsely accused of fathering a child, she bore the slander in silence until her sex was discovered only after her death.
Notes: One commemoration of the daughter (Mary/Marinus) and her father Eugene. 'Slander / False Accusation' is supported by her recorded life.

Name: Saint Anthony, Patriarch of Constantinople
Also Known As: Anthony II Kauleas; Antony of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Anthony, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Patriarch of Constantinople who worked to heal the divisions in the Church and was renowned for his asceticism and peacemaking; reposed in 895.

Name: Saint Kristo the Gardener of Albania
Also Known As: Christos the Gardener; Kristo of Albania
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Kristo, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Albanian gardener under Ottoman rule who was falsely accused of promising to convert to Islam and, refusing to deny Christ, was martyred in 1748.

Name: Saint Prochorus of Georgia
Also Known As: Prochorus the Georgian; Prochorus of the Cross Monastery
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Prochorus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Georgian monk who founded and built the Monastery of the Holy Cross in Jerusalem in the eleventh century.

Name: Martyr Nicholas Dvali of Jerusalem
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Venerable-Martyr Nicholas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Georgian monk of the Holy Cross Monastery in Jerusalem who, after enduring imprisonment and torture, was beheaded for refusing to renounce Christ in 1314.

Name: Venerable Bassian of Uglich
Also Known As: Bassian of Ryabovo; Vassian of Uglich
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Bassian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of St. Paisius of Uglich who founded the Ryabovo Monastery near Uglich; reposed in 1509.

Name: Martyr Luke of Jerusalem, the Georgian
Also Known As: Luka of Jerusalem; Luke the Georgian
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Venerable-Martyr Luke, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Georgian monk of Jerusalem who confessed Christ and was martyred under Muslim rule in the thirteenth century.

Name: Saint Martinian of Caesarea in Palestine
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Martinian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A hermit near Caesarea who, fiercely tempted, fled all occasions of sin even to a desert island and a wandering life, becoming a pattern of struggle against the passions; granted gifts of healing and casting out demons.
Notes: Commemorated with Sts. Zoe and Photina, whose conversions are bound to his life.

Name: Saint Zoe of Caesarea in Palestine
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Mother Zoe, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A harlot who came to tempt St. Martinian but, struck by his words and example, repented utterly and spent the rest of her life in penitence as a nun.
Notes: Commemorated with St. Martinian of Caesarea.

Name: Saint Photina of Caesarea in Palestine
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Mother Photina, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A woman who took St. Martinian's place on his desert island after he departed, living there in solitary asceticism until her repose.
Notes: Commemorated with St. Martinian of Caesarea. Not to be confused with St. Photini the Samaritan Woman.

Name: Venerable Symeon the Myrrh-gusher of Serbia
Also Known As: Stephen Nemanja; Simeon the Myrrh-streaming; Stefan Nemanja
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Symeon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Stefan Nemanja, the grand prince and founder of the Serbian state, who abdicated to become the monk Simeon on Mount Athos, where with his son St. Sava he founded Hilandar Monastery; after his repose his relics streamed fragrant myrrh.
Notes: Father of St. Sava I of Serbia. Principal commemoration of the Serbian Nemanjic dynasty's founder-saint.

Name: Saint Eulogius, Archbishop of Alexandria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Eulogius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Patriarch of Alexandria and a learned defender of Orthodoxy against heresy, a friend and correspondent of St. Gregory the Dialogist; reposed in the early seventh century.

Name: Saint George Konissky, Archbishop of Mogilev
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch George, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Archbishop of Mogilev in Belarus, a defender of Orthodox Christians against forced union under Polish rule, preacher and writer; reposed in 1795.

Name: Venerable Auxentius of Bithynia
Also Known As: Auxentius of the Mountain; Auxentios of Bithynia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Auxentius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Syrian who served at the court of Theodosius the Younger, then withdrew to a mountain in Bithynia as a hermit; sought out for his counsel and gift of healing, he took part in the Council of Chalcedon. Reposed around 470.

Name: Venerable Isaac the Recluse of the Kiev Caves
Also Known As: Isaac of the Kiev Near Caves; Chern of Toropets
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Isaac, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A wealthy merchant of Toropets who gave away his goods and became a recluse in the Kiev Caves; deceived and grievously assailed by demons, he recovered through humility and obedience and ended his life in great virtue. Reposed 1090.

Name: The Twelve Greek Master-Builders of the Kiev Caves
Also Known As: The Twelve Greeks who built the Dormition Cathedral of the Kiev Caves Lavra
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Fathers, the Twelve Builders, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Twelve Greek master-builders sent miraculously from Constantinople to raise the great Dormition Cathedral of the Kiev Caves Lavra; they remained as monks and were buried there. Circa 1073.
Notes: One commemoration of the twelve unnamed Greek builders and iconographers of the Kiev Caves.

Name: Venerable Maron the Hermit of Syria
Also Known As: Maron of Cyrrhus; Maro the Hermit
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Maron, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A hermit who lived in the open air on a mountain near Cyrrhus in Syria, renowned for healing soul and body; a teacher of many ascetics and praised by St. John Chrysostom. Reposed before 423.

Name: Saint Abraham, Bishop of Charres in Mesopotamia
Also Known As: Abraham of Carrhae; Abraham of Cyrrhus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Abraham, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An ascetic of Cyrrhus who, posing as a merchant, converted a pagan village in Lebanon by his patience and charity, and later became bishop of Charres (Carrhae); honored at the court of Theodosius. Reposed in the early fifth century.

Name: Saint Hilarion the New of Georgia
Also Known As: Hilarion Kartveli; Hilarion the Georgian; Hilarion Akhali
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Hilarion, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Georgian hieroschemamonk who labored on Mount Athos, in Jerusalem, and in Russia, renowned as a clairvoyant elder and spiritual guide; reposed in 1864 and glorified in 2002.

Name: Apostle Onesimus of the Seventy
Also Known As: Onesimus of Byzantium; Onesimus the runaway slave; Onesimos
Life Experience: Slavery; Imprisonment; Repentance from grave sin
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostle Onesimus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A runaway slave of Philemon of Colossae, Onesimus fled to Rome where the Apostle Paul converted and baptized him; Paul's Epistle to Philemon commends him as a brother. He later served the apostles, preached in many lands, and became a bishop, succeeding the Apostle Timothy at Ephesus. He was martyred under Trajan by stoning and beheading around 109.
Notes: One of the Seventy Apostles; the recipient and subject of St Paul's Epistle to Philemon.

Name: Venerable Paphnutius the Recluse of the Kiev Caves
Also Known As: Paphnutius the Hermit of the Kiev Caves; Pafnuty
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Paphnutius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An ascetic recluse of the Kiev Caves Lavra, remembered for his strict reclusion and the gift of tears.

Name: Venerable Paphnutius and his daughter Euphrosyne of Alexandria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Fathers and Mothers, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A father and daughter of fifth-century Alexandria who both forsook the world for the monastic life.
Notes: Commemorated together as one entry by the OCA Synaxarion.

Name: Venerable Eusebius the Hermit of Syria
Also Known As: Eusebius of Asikha; Eusebios the Hermit
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Eusebius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A solitary hermit of fourth-century Syria who lived a life of extreme asceticism and reposed at the age of ninety.

Name: Saint Anthimus of Chios
Also Known As: Anthimos of Chios; Anthimus the New; Argyrios Vagianos
Commonly Asked Intercessions: Mental Health; Healing; Deliverance from the Occult
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Anthimus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest-monk of the island of Chios (1869-1960) who founded the convent of Panagia Voetheia and was renowned as a healer of the sick and the demon-afflicted. He was glorified by the Ecumenical Patriarchate in 1992.
Notes: Glorified by the Ecumenical Patriarchate of Constantinople in 1992.

Name: Martyr Pamphilius the Presbyter and his Companions at Caesarea
Also Known As: Pamphilus of Caesarea; Valens the Deacon; Paul; Porphyrios; Seleukos; Theodoulos; Julian; Samuel; Elias; Daniel; Jeremiah; Isaiah; the Twelve Martyrs of Caesarea in Palestine
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Pamphilius and Companions, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A learned priest of Caesarea in Palestine and friend of the historian Eusebius, Pamphilius founded a great Christian library and copied the Scriptures. He and eleven companions — clergy, soldiers, servants, and youths — were martyred under Diocletian for confessing Christ.
Notes: One commemoration of twelve martyrs: Pamphilius the Presbyter, Valens the Deacon, Paul, Porphyrios, Seleukos, Theodoulos, Julian, Samuel, Elias, Daniel, Jeremiah, and Isaiah.

Name: Saint Maruthas, Bishop of Martyropolis
Also Known As: Maruthas of Tagrit; Maruthas of Mesopotamia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Maruthas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Tagrit (Martyropolis) on the Byzantine-Persian frontier, skilled in medicine, who took part in the Second Ecumenical Council and gathered the relics of the Persian martyrs, for which his city was named Martyropolis. He reposed around 422.
Notes: Commemorated with the Persian Martyrs whose relics he gathered at Martyropolis.

Name: Saint Flavian, Archbishop of Antioch
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Flavian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Archbishop of Antioch and contemporary of Saint John Chrysostom, who interceded with the Emperor Theodosius to spare the citizens of Antioch after their revolt.

Name: New Hieromartyr Elias of Moscow
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Hieromartyr Elias, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Russian priest educated at the Moscow Theological Academy, arrested by the Soviet secret police in 1932 and killed in a concentration-camp fire together with eleven other Christians.
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: Hieromartyr Hermogenes, Patriarch of Moscow
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Hermogenes, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Patriarch of Moscow during the Time of Troubles who refused to bless the Polish occupation and roused the Russian people to defend the faith. Imprisoned by the invaders, he died of starvation in 1612. Glorified in 1913.
Notes: Principal feast Feb 17 (repose); also commemorated May 12 (glorification).

Name: Venerable Theodore the Silent of the Kiev Caves
Also Known As: Theodore the Silent of the Far Caves; Theodore the Taciturn
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Theodore, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Kiev Caves who practiced the discipline of silence and was granted wonderworking gifts.

Name: Saint Mariamne the Apostolic Virgin
Also Known As: Mariamne, sister of the Apostle Philip; Mary
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Mariamne, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Sister of the Apostle Philip, she shared in his apostolic labors and continued preaching the Gospel in Lykaonia after his death.

Name: Saint Joseph of Dionysiou
Also Known As: Joseph the Iconographer; New Martyr Joseph of Dionysiou
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Joseph, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An iconographer and monk of the Dionysiou Monastery on Mount Athos, hanged in 1819 after refusing to convert to Islam.

Name: Saint Auxibius, Bishop of Soli in Cyprus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Auxibius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Established as bishop of Soli in Cyprus by the Apostle Mark, he served the Church there for some fifty years until his repose around 102.

Name: New Martyr Theodore the Byzantine
Also Known As: Theodore the New Martyr of Constantinople; Theodore the returned Christian
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Theodore, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Having lapsed from the faith, he returned to Christ and was hanged by the Turkish authorities in 1795 for confessing Christianity.

Name: Saint Theodosius the Bulgarian and his disciple Romanus
Also Known As: Theodosius of Tarnovo; Theodosius of Trnovo; Romanos; Romanus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Fathers Theodosius and Romanus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of Saint Gregory of Sinai, Theodosius founded a monastery near Trnovo and was a zealous defender of Orthodoxy against heresy. He reposed at Constantinople in 1362, his disciple Romanus succeeding him as abbot.
Notes: Commemorated together with his disciple Romanus.

Name: New Hieromartyr Nicholas Kandaurov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Hieromartyr Nicholas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Russian priest who suffered for Christ under the Soviet persecution of the Church.
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia

Name: Saint Finan of Lindisfarne
Also Known As: Finan of Lindisfarne; Finan the Bishop
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Finan, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Irish monk of Iona who succeeded Saint Aidan as bishop of Lindisfarne, advancing the conversion of the English and reposing in 661.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint of the Celtic tradition; details limited in the OCA listing.

Name: Hieromartyr Theodore of Atchara
Also Known As: Theodore of Adchara; Theodore the Georgian Hieromartyr
Life Experience: Persecution; Exile; Repentance from grave sin
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Theodore, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born to a Georgian family forced into Islam, he freed himself, embraced Christianity, and became a monk and deputy abbot at a Georgian monastery in Smyrna. While journeying to Mount Athos in 1822 he was seized and executed by Ottoman soldiers. Canonized by the Georgian Orthodox Church in 2002.
Notes: Canonized by the Georgian Apostolic Orthodox Church in 2002.

Name: Saint Leo the Great, Pope of Rome
Also Known As: Leo I; Pope Leo the Great; Leo of Rome
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Leo, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Pope of Rome (440-461) and great defender of Orthodox doctrine, whose Tome upheld the two natures of Christ and was acclaimed at the Fourth Ecumenical Council of Chalcedon in 451. He also turned back Attila the Hun from Rome.
Notes: His Tome to Flavian was foundational to the Council of Chalcedon.
Works by the Saint: Tome of Leo; Sermons; Letters

Name: Venerable Cosmas of Yakhrom
Also Known As: Cosmas of Yakhroma; Kosmas of Yakhrom
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Cosmas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: After a vision of an icon of the Dormition, he founded a monastery on the Yakhrom River and served as its abbot, reposing in 1492.

Name: Saint Agapitus the Confessor, Bishop of Synnada
Also Known As: Agapitos of Synnada; Agapetus the Wonderworker of Synnada
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Agapitus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk and later soldier who confessed Christ under Diocletian and Maximian, he became bishop of Synnada in Phrygia and was renowned for healing the sick and working many miracles.

Name: Saint Flavian the Confessor, Patriarch of Constantinople
Also Known As: Flavian of Constantinople; Flavianos the Confessor
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Flavian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Patriarch of Constantinople who upheld Orthodox doctrine against the heresy of Eutyches and was violently abused at the "Robber Council" of Ephesus in 449, dying of his injuries shortly after.
Notes: Recipient of the Tome of Saint Leo the Great.

Name: Saint Nicholas, Catholicos of Georgia
Also Known As: Nicholas I of Georgia; Nicholas Batonishvili
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Nicholas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Son of King Levan I of Kakheti, he forsook royal estate for the monastic life and was enthroned as Catholicos of All Georgia in 1584, corresponding with the Russian Patriarch.

Name: Saint Colman of Lindisfarne
Also Known As: Colman of Lindisfarne; Colman the Bishop
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Colman, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Irish monk of Iona who became bishop of Lindisfarne, upheld the Celtic usages at the Synod of Whitby, and withdrew to Ireland to found monasteries, reposing in 676.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint of the Celtic tradition; details limited in the OCA listing.

Name: Saint Theodore of Serbia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Saint Theodore, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A saint venerated in the Serbian tradition; details are not preserved in the OCA listing.
Notes: Honest stub; no biographical detail available in the OCA Synaxarion.

Name: Apostles Archippus and Philemon of the Seventy, and Martyr Apphia
Also Known As: Archippus; Philemon of Colossae; Apphia; the household of Colossae
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostles Archippus and Philemon and Martyr Apphia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Companions of the Apostle Paul at Colossae — Philemon, to whom Paul addressed an epistle, his wife Apphia, and Archippus — who hosted the Church in their home and suffered martyrdom under Nero.
Notes: Commemorated together as one entry; also commemorated Nov 22.

Name: Martyrs Maximus, Theodotus, Hesychius, and Asclepiodota of Adrianopolis
Also Known As: Maximus; Theodotus; Hesychius; Asclepiodote; the Martyrs of Adrianople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Maximus, Theodotus, Hesychius, and Asclepiodota, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Four Christians of Adrianopolis who confessed Christ and were martyred during the persecution under the Emperor Maximian.
Notes: Commemorated together as one entry.

Name: Confessors Eugene and Macarius, Presbyters at Antioch
Also Known As: Eugene; Macarius; the Confessors of Antioch
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Confessors Eugene and Macarius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two priests of Antioch who boldly opposed Julian the Apostate and were exiled to a desert oasis in Arabia, where they reposed.
Notes: Commemorated together as one entry.

Name: Venerable Dositheus of Palestine
Also Known As: Dositheus of Gaza; Dositheos, disciple of Abba Dorotheus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Dositheus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A former soldier who became the disciple of Abba Dorotheus of Gaza and a model of perfect obedience, tending the sick of the monastery though himself afflicted with illness.
Notes: A pattern of monastic obedience drawn from the writings of Abba Dorotheus.

Name: Saint Rabulas of Samosata
Also Known As: Rabulas the Syrian; Rabbula of Samosata
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Rabulas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Syrian monk and builder of monasteries who reposed around 530.

Name: Venerable Theodore of Sanaxar
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Theodore, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A guards officer who left worldly honor for the monastic life, becoming abbot and restorer of the Sanaxar Monastery (1719-1791). He was uncle to the righteous admiral Theodore Ushakov.

Name: Venerable Conon, Abbot of Penthucla
Also Known As: Conon of Penthucla; Conan of the Pethukla Monastery
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Conon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A desert ascetic and abbot of the Penthucla Monastery near the Jordan River, who reposed around 555.

Name: Venerable-Martyr Philothea of Athens
Also Known As: Philothei of Athens; Philothea the Athenian; Revoula Benizelos
Life Experience: Widowhood; Difficult Marriage; Persecution; Torture; Slavery
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Venerable-Martyr Philothea, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Athenian noblewoman (1522-1589) who, after an unhappy marriage and widowhood, founded a convent, schools, and refuges, and ransomed Christian women from slavery. Beaten by the Turks for her labors, she died of her wounds and is honored as a martyr.
Notes: A patroness of the city of Athens.

Name: Saint Leo, Bishop of Catania in Sicily
Also Known As: Leo of Catania; Leo the Wonderworker of Catania
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Leo, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Catania in Sicily, born at Ravenna, famed for his charity to the poor, his healing miracles, and his confrontation with the sorcerer Heliodorus.

Name: Venerable Agathon, Wonderworker of the Kiev Caves
Also Known As: Agathon of the Kiev Caves; Agathon the Wonderworker
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Agathon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A great ascetic of the Kiev Caves who healed the sick by the laying on of his hands and foresaw the hour of his own repose.

Name: Hieromartyr Cornelius, Abbot of the Pskov Caves
Also Known As: Cornelius of Pskov; Kornily of the Pechory Monastery
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Cornelius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Abbot of the Pskov Caves Monastery for over forty years, who greatly expanded it and spread the faith. He was slain by Ivan the Terrible in 1570.

Name: Hieromartyr Sadoc, Bishop of Persia, and the 128 Martyrs with him
Also Known As: Sadoth; Sadok of Persia; the 128 Martyrs of Persia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Sadoc and Companions, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Successor to the Hieromartyr Simeon as bishop in Persia, Sadoc was martyred with one hundred and twenty-eight clergy and faithful, including nine virgins, under King Sapor II.
Notes: Commemorated together as one entry of 129 martyrs.

Name: Saint Agathon, Pope of Rome
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Agathon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Pope of Rome (679-682) under whom the Sixth Ecumenical Council was convened, upholding Orthodox doctrine against the Monothelite heresy.

Name: Monastic Martyrs of Valaam
Also Known As: The 34 Martyrs of Valaam Monastery; the Valaam Martyrs
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs of Valaam, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Thirty-four monks of Valaam Monastery — eighteen elders and sixteen novices — slain by Lutheran Swedish forces on February 20, 1578 for refusing to abandon the Orthodox faith. They were canonized by the Russian Church in 2000.
Notes: Canonized by the Russian Orthodox Church Jubilee Council in 2000.

Name: Venerable Timothy of Symbola in Bithynia
Also Known As: Timothy of Symbola; Timothy the Sigriane
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Timothy, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An ascetic monk of the monastery of Symbola in Bithynia who pursued the solitary life and reposed around 795.

Name: Saint Eustathius, Archbishop of Antioch
Also Known As: Eustathius the Great; Eustathios of Antioch; Eustace of Antioch
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Eustathius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Archbishop of Antioch and a leading defender of Orthodoxy at the First Ecumenical Council against the Arians, for which he was deposed and exiled, reposing around 337.
Notes: A father of the First Ecumenical Council of Nicaea.

Name: Saint George, Bishop of Amastris
Also Known As: George of Amastris; George the Bishop of the Black Sea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch George, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Amastris on the Black Sea, renowned for his miracles and his composition of liturgical canons, who protected his city and reposed around 805.

Name: Saint John Scholasticus, Patriarch of Constantinople
Also Known As: John III Scholasticus; John the Scholastic; John of Antioch
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A canon lawyer who became Patriarch of Constantinople (565-577) and compiled an influential collection of Church canons and civil laws.

Name: Saint Zacharias, Patriarch of Jerusalem
Also Known As: Zacharias of Jerusalem; Zachariah the Patriarch
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Zacharias, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Patriarch of Jerusalem who was carried off into Persian captivity together with the Precious Cross during the invasion of Chosroes, and later returned to his see. He reposed around 633.

Name: Holy Martyrs at the Gate of Eugenius in Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs at the Gate of Eugenius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A company of early Christian martyrs of Constantinople whose names are unknown but written in the Book of Life; their relics were discovered near the Gate of Eugenius and were the source of many healings.
Notes: Names not recorded.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Maurice and those with him
Also Known As: St. Maurice the military commander; Photinus his son; Theodore; Philip; the Seventy Soldiers of Apamea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Maurice and your companions, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A military commander at Apamea in Syria who, with his son Photinus and seventy of his soldiers, confessed Christ under the emperor Maximian Galerius. After cruel tortures they were left bound in a marsh to be devoured by insects and so received the crown of martyrdom (c. 305).
Notes: Commemorated as one company with his son Photinus and the soldiers Theodore and Philip among the seventy.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Thalassius and Limnaeus of Syria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Fathers Thalassius and Limnaeus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Syrian ascetics of the fifth century. Thalassius spent some thirty-eight years in stillness on a hill near the village of Targala; his disciple Limnaeus lived in open-air asceticism, bearing illness with patience, and later was joined to Saint Maron.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Baradates the Hermit of Syria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Baradates, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Syrian ascetic near Antioch who practiced extreme austerity, enduring heat and cold in a small enclosure, and was praised by Theodoret of Cyrus for his wisdom. He reposed about the year 460.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Athanasius the Confessor
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Confessor Athanasius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A confessor who suffered for the veneration of the holy icons under the iconoclast emperor Leo the Armenian, enduring imprisonment and hardship for the Orthodox faith. He reposed about the year 821.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Nine Martyred Children of Kola
Also Known As: Guram; Adarnerse; Baqar; Vache; Bardzim; Dachi; Juansher; Ramaz; Parsman; the Nine Martyred Brothers of Kola
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs of Kola, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Nine young brothers of the village of Kola in the Georgian region of Tao who, refusing to renounce Christ, were martyred together by their own pagan kinsmen and townspeople and buried in a single grave.
Notes: Commemorated as one group; individual names listed in Also Known As.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Polycarp, Bishop of Smyrna
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Polycarp, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of the Apostle John and bishop of Smyrna, one of the Apostolic Fathers. An aged shepherd of his flock, he was arrested in persecution and, refusing to deny Christ, was burned at the stake and pierced; his martyrdom (c. 167) is among the earliest recorded.
Works by the Saint: Epistle to the Philippians
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Polycarp of Briansk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Polycarp, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Formerly Prince Peter Boryatinsky, he forsook the world for monastic life and founded the Transfiguration Monastery at Briansk, where he labored in humility until his repose (c. 1620-1621).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Ascetics of the Syrian Deserts
Also Known As: John; Antiochus; Antoninus; Moses; Zebinas; Polychronius; Damian; the Syrian Desert ascetics
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Fathers of the Syrian deserts, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A company of fifth-century Syrian ascetics described by Theodoret of Cyrus: John, who lived shelterless for twenty-five years, with Antiochus and Antoninus; Moses; Zebinas, who never sat during prayer; Polychronius; and Damian. They strove together in stillness and unceasing prayer.
Notes: Commemorated as one group of ascetics; individual names in Also Known As.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Alexander, Founder of the Monastery of the Unsleeping Ones
Also Known As: Alexander the Akoimetos; Alexander the Sleepless
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Alexander, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Founder of the monastery of the 'Unsleeping Ones' (Akoimetoi), where the monks were divided into choirs so that the divine praises were chanted without ceasing day and night. He reposed about the year 430. Also commemorated July 3.
Notes: Principal feast Jul 3 in some calendars; also commemorated Feb 23.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Damian of Esphigmenou of Mount Athos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Damian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A hesychast of the Esphigmenou Monastery on Mount Athos who labored in stillness; a sweet fragrance is said to have issued from his grave after his repose about the year 1280.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable-Martyr Damian of Philotheou
Also Known As: Damianos of Philotheou; Damian the New Martyr
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Venerable-Martyr Damian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of Philotheou on Mount Athos who preached repentance in the region of Agrapha during the Turkish yoke. Seized for his preaching, he was tortured and hanged, and his body burned, receiving the crown of martyrdom in 1568.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Righteous Gorgonia, Sister of Saint Gregory the Theologian
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Righteous Gorgonia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Daughter of Saints Gregory the Elder and Nonna and sister of Saint Gregory the Theologian, who preached her funeral oration. A devout wife and mother of five, she was renowned for her piety, charity, and chastity, and reposed about the year 370.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Hieromartyr Nicholas (Dmitrov) of Tver
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Hieromartyr Nicholas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest of the Tver region who suffered for Christ during the persecution of the Church in Russia in the twentieth century.
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Erasmus of the Kiev Caves
Also Known As: Erasmus of the Near Caves; Erasmus of the Kiev Near Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Erasmus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Kiev Near Caves who spent all his wealth adorning the church with icons. Falling into poverty and despondency, he was near despair, but the Mother of God appeared to him; he repented, was tonsured to the great schema, and reposed in peace. Also commemorated September 28 with the Fathers of the Near Caves.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Tarasius, Patriarch of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Tarasius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Raised from the lay state to the patriarchal throne of Constantinople in 784, he presided over the Seventh Ecumenical Council (787) which restored the veneration of the holy icons. He was known for his asceticism, charity to the poor, and steadfastness before imperial pressure, and reposed in 806.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Porphyrius, Archbishop of Gaza
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Porphyrius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: After ascetic struggles in Egypt and the Holy Land, he became bishop of Gaza, then largely pagan. Through prayer, preaching, and miracles he overthrew the idols and brought many to Christ, shepherding his flock some twenty-five years until his repose about 420.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Sebastian of Poshekhonye
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Sebastian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monastic founder who established a monastery on the River Sokhota in the Poshekhonye region and labored there in asceticism, reposing about the year 1500. Also commemorated December 18.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Christodoulos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Christodoulos, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A martyr who was slain by the sword for confessing Christ; little is recorded of his life beyond his witness unto death.
Notes: Few biographical details preserved.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Martyr John Kalphes
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A cabinetmaker of Galata in Constantinople who, refusing to deny Christ and embrace Islam, was beheaded for his faith in 1575 during the Turkish yoke.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Prokopios the Confessor of Decapolis
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Prokopios, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Decapolis who defended the veneration of the holy icons during the iconoclast persecution, suffering imprisonment and torture for the Orthodox faith. He reposed in peace after the triumph of Orthodoxy.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Titus the Presbyter of the Kiev Caves
Also Known As: Titus the Priest of the Kiev Near Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Titus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A hieromonk of the Kiev Near Caves who, after a bitter quarrel with another monk, was reconciled on what seemed his deathbed; through forgiveness he was healed and lived out his days in humility, a witness to the power of reconciliation.
Notes: Often invoked for the healing of enmity and the reconciliation of those at strife.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Titus the Soldier of the Kiev Caves
Also Known As: Titus the Former Soldier of the Kiev Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Titus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A former soldier wounded in battle who, leaving the world, entered the Kiev Caves and pleased God by his monastic struggles and repentance.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Thalelaeus the Hermit of Syria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Thalelaeus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Syrian ascetic who enclosed himself in a small hut and wept continually for his sins. Numbered among the unmercenary physicians, he healed the sick and converted many pagans, reposing about the year 460.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saints Asclepius and James of Syria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Fathers Asclepius and James, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two Syrian hermits who struggled in stillness near the village of Nimuza, described among the ascetics of the region by Theodoret of Cyrus.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Righteous Stephen of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Righteous Stephen, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A former courtier of Constantinople who devoted his wealth and life to founding and serving a hospice for the aged and infirm, reposing about the year 614.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Julian, Eunus, Besas and Macarius of Alexandria
Also Known As: Julian; Eunos (Kronion); Beza (Besas) the soldier; Mekaros (Macarius)
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Julian, Eunus, Besas and Macarius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Martyrs of Alexandria under the emperor Decius. Julian, crippled and unable to walk, was carried to his confession; his servant Eunus and the soldier Besas, who defended them, and Macarius were all put to death for Christ.
Notes: Commemorated together as one company of martyrs.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Leander, Bishop of Seville
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Leander, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Seville and brother of Saints Isidore, Fulgentius and Florentina. He labored to bring the Visigoths from Arianism to the Orthodox faith and reformed the worship of the Spanish Church, reposing about the year 600.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Basil the Confessor
Also Known As: Basil, companion of Procopius of Decapolis
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Basil, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk and companion of Saint Prokopios of Decapolis who suffered for the veneration of the holy icons under the iconoclast emperor Leo the Isaurian, enduring torment for the faith and reposing in peace about the year 750.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Blessed Nicholas of Pskov, Fool-for-Christ
Short Prayer (Intercession): Blessed Nicholas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A fool-for-Christ of Pskov who labored in this hard ascetic feat for over thirty years, receiving gifts of wonderworking and prophecy. He is famed for boldly rebuking Tsar Ivan the Terrible and so sparing the city of Pskov from the slaughter visited upon Novgorod. He reposed in 1576.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Proterius, Patriarch of Alexandria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Proterius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Patriarch of Alexandria who upheld the Orthodox faith of the Council of Chalcedon against the Monophysites. He was set upon by a heretical mob and slain on Great and Holy Saturday in 457.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Nestor, Bishop of Magydos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Nestor, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Magydos in Pamphylia who gave himself up to the authorities to spare his flock during the persecution of Decius. After confessing Christ under torture, he was crucified about the year 250.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Marina and Kyra of Syria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mothers Marina and Kyra, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two sisters of Berea in Syria who, leaving their noble home, enclosed themselves in a small open courtyard bound with heavy chains, keeping silence and fasting for some forty years. They reposed about the year 450.
Notes: Commemorated together as sisters.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Domnica of Syria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Domnica, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Syrian ascetic and companion in struggle of Saints Marina and Kyra, who built a small hut and wept continually in prayer, eating only lentils, reposing about the year 460.
Notes: Distinct from Venerable Domnica of Constantinople (Jan 8).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable John Cassian the Roman
Also Known As: John Cassian; Cassian the Roman; Ioannes Kassianos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father John Cassian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born in Lesser Scythia, he embraced monastic life in Bethlehem and Egypt, learning from the desert fathers, then settled in Gaul where he founded monasteries at Marseilles. His Institutes and Conferences transmitted the wisdom of the Egyptian fathers to the West, including the teaching on the eight passions. He reposed about the year 435.
Notes: Leap-day saint: commemorated Feb 29; in non-leap years his commemoration is transferred to Feb 28.
Works by the Saint: The Institutes of the Cenobitic Life; The Conferences of the Fathers
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint John-Barsanuphius, Bishop of Damascus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch John-Barsanuphius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Damascus who, drawn to stillness, withdrew from his see and lived as an ascetic in the Nitrian desert of Egypt, pleasing God in humility and prayer.
Notes: Leap-day saint: in non-leap years the commemoration transfers to Feb 28.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Theoktistus, Abbot of Pelekete
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Theoktistus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Abbot of the Pelekete monastery who suffered for the holy icons under the iconoclast emperor Constantine Copronymus, being tormented with boiling tar and receiving the crown of martyrdom.
Notes: Leap-day saint: in non-leap years the commemoration transfers to Feb 28.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Leo of Cappadocia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Leo, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A martyr who offered himself in exchange for captive monks, laying down his life out of love for the brethren.
Notes: Leap-day saint: in non-leap years the commemoration transfers to Feb 28.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Germanus of Dacia Pontica
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Germanus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Companion and spiritual brother of Saint John Cassian, with whom he journeyed to the monasteries of Bethlehem and the deserts of Egypt to learn from the fathers; he was ordained priest and shared in Cassian's labors.
Notes: Leap-day saint: in non-leap years the commemoration transfers to Feb 28.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Eudokia of Heliopolis
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Venerable-Martyr Eudokia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Samaritan woman of Heliopolis in Phoenicia who had led a sinful and worldly life before her conversion to Christ, after which she gave her wealth to the poor, embraced monastic asceticism, and was beheaded for her faith.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrius of Zelenets
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Martyrius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born Menas and orphaned young, he was raised by a priest and became a monastic ascetic, founding the Holy Trinity monastery at Zelenets near Veliki Luki in Russia.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Nestor, Tribimius, Marcellus, and Anthony of Perge
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Christian preachers at Perge in Pamphylia who fearlessly proclaimed Christ and were put to death during the persecution under the emperor Decius.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Antonina of Nicea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Antonina, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian woman of Nicea in Bithynia who endured fierce tortures and imprisonment, remaining steadfast in refusing to offer sacrifice to idols during the reign of Maximian.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Marcellus and Anthony
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two martyrs who were committed to the flames for their confession of Christ, their faith proving unbreakable through the ordeal of fire.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Domnina the Younger of Syria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Domnina, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A virgin ascetic of Cyrrhus in Syria who lived in great austerity in a small garden hut, and whose holy life was recorded by Blessed Theodoret of Cyrrhus.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Agapius of Vatopedi
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Agapius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monastic novice of the Vatopedi monastery on Mount Athos who was captured by raiders and carried into slavery, and is remembered as an exemplar of monastic obedience.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: David of Wales
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch David, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop and missionary of Wales who founded monasteries and a strict ascetic rule, became the chief shepherd of the Welsh church, and is honored as the patron saint of Wales.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Theodotus of Cyrenia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Theodotus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop in Cyprus who preached against idolatry and suffered for the faith during the persecution under the emperor Licinius in the early fourth century.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Arsenius of Tver
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Arsenius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Monk of the Kiev Caves monastery who became Bishop of Tver, known for his piety and strict observance of monastic discipline; reposed in 1409.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Euthalia of Sicily
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Euthalia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young woman of Leontini in Sicily whose mother was healed through the saints and led the family toward Christ; she was martyred for confessing the faith.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Troadius of Neocaesarea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Troadius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young man of Neocaesarea in Pontus who endured torture and was martyred under the emperor Decius; his suffering was revealed in a vision to Saint Gregory the Wonderworker.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Agathon of Egypt
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Agathon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A desert ascetic of Egypt and contemporary of Saint Macarius, remembered for his extraordinary meekness and spiritual discernment.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: 400 Martyrs Slain by the Lombards in Sicily
Also Known As: Sanctulus the Presbyter; Hospicius the Hermit
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A company of Christians in Sicily put to death by the Lombards for refusing to worship idols; among them the names of the presbyter Sanctulus and the hermit Hospicius are preserved.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Sabbatius of Tver
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Sabbatius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Russian monastic who, with the blessing of Saint Arsenius of Tver, founded a strict monastery near Tver whose discipline influenced later ascetics.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Joachim of Ithaka
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Joachim, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born on the Greek island of Ithaka in 1786, he endured a difficult childhood and later embraced the monastic and ascetic life.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Eutropius, Cleonicus, and Basiliscus of Amasea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Eutropius, Cleonicus, and Basiliscus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Brothers Eutropius and Cleonicus, with Basiliscus the nephew of the Great Martyr Theodore the Recruit, confessed Christ and suffered together at Amasea in Pontus under Diocletian.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Piamoun the Virgin
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Piamoun, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Egyptian virgin raised in piety by her Christian mother, she lived an ascetic life of fasting and prayer and was numbered among the desert saints recorded in the Lausiac History.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saints Zenon and Zoilus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Zenon and Zoilus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two saints commemorated together in the Byzantine Synaxarion, of whom little is recorded.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint John (Chrysostom) IV, Catholicos of Georgia
Also Known As: John IV of Georgia; John Chrysostom IV
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Catholicos of Georgia at the close of the tenth century, whose parents had prayed for his birth at the tomb of Saint Shio of Mgvime; he shepherded the Georgian Church into the new millennium.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Gerasimus of the Jordan
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Gerasimus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A fifth-century ascetic from Lycia in Asia Minor who, after training in the Egyptian desert, settled by the Jordan River and founded a monastic community. He is famous for the story of taming a lion by removing a thorn from its paw.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Gerasimus of Vologda
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Gerasimus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk tonsured at the Kiev Caves who travelled north and laboured as the first monastic settler and wonderworker of the Vologda region of Russia.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Joasaph of Snetogorsk
Also Known As: Joasaph of Pskov; Ioasaph of Snetogorsk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Joasaph, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Abbot of the Snetogorsk monastery near Pskov who was slain together with his monks during a raid by German forces in the thirteenth century.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Right-believing Prince Basil (Vasilko) of Rostov
Also Known As: Vasilko of Rostov; Basil of Rostov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Prince Basil, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A prince of Rostov captured by the Mongols after the battle on the Sit River in 1238 who was killed for refusing to renounce his faith and serve his captors.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Right-believing Prince Daniel of Moscow
Also Known As: Daniel of Moscow; Daniil of Moscow
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Prince Daniel, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The youngest son of Saint Alexander Nevsky and founder of the Moscow princely line, who ruled with humility, established monasteries, and took monastic vows before his death in 1303.
Notes: Principal feast is Mar 17 (also commemorated Aug 30, translation of relics). Mar 4 is a secondary commemoration.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Martyrs Paul and Juliana of Ptolemais
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Paul and Juliana, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A brother and sister of Ptolemais in Phoenicia who were tortured and put to death for confessing Christ during the reign of the emperor Aurelian.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: James the Faster of Phoenicia
Also Known As: James the Ascetic; Jacob the Faster
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father James, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Syrian ascetic who lived enclosed in a cave for many years and received the gift of working wonders, but who, after a grievous fall, did profound penance and was restored.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Right-believing Prince Wenceslas of the Czech Lands
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Prince Wenceslas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A tenth-century duke of Bohemia raised in piety by his grandmother Saint Ludmila, murdered at the church door by his brother's men; March 4 commemorates the translation of his relics.
Notes: Principal feast is Sep 28 (martyrdom); Mar 4 is the translation of relics. Flag for reconciliation if already present at Sep 28.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Gregory of Constantia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Gregory, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of Constantia in Cyprus, known from an early liturgical codex; few details of his life survive.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Vyacheslav (Leontiev) the Priest
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Vyacheslav, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Russian priest who suffered martyrdom during the Soviet persecutions of the twentieth century; few biographical details survive.
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Conon of Isauria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Conon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A native of Isauria in Asia Minor who from his youth was devoted to Christ and, according to his life, aided by the Archangel Michael. He confessed the faith and suffered martyrdom.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Conon the Gardener
Also Known As: Conon the Gardener; Conon of Pamphylia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Conon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born in Nazareth, he lived quietly tending a garden in Pamphylia. He was seized and tortured during the persecution under Emperor Decius and died confessing Christ.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Onesimus of Isauria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Onesimus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A martyr beheaded by the sword for confessing his faith in Christ.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Virgin-Martyr Irais of Antinoe
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Virgin-Martyr Irais, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A virgin martyr of Antinoe in Egypt. Little detail of her life survives beyond her witness unto death.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Eulogius of Palestine
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Eulogius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A man who gave his inherited wealth to the poor and wandered preaching Christ and converting pagans, until he was arrested during persecution and martyred.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Eulampius of Palestine
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Eulampius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A martyr of Palestine beheaded for his faith in Christ.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Mark the Ascetic
Also Known As: Mark the Faster; Mark the Hermit; Mark the Monk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Mark, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born in Athens, he became a monk in the desert of Egypt and was renowned for his ascetic life and knowledge of Scripture, said to know the whole Bible by heart. Several of his ascetic and theological discourses survive.
Works by the Saint: On Those Who Think They Are Made Righteous by Works; On the Spiritual Law
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Hesychius the Faster of Bithynia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Hesychius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born in the eighth century in a coastal town of Bithynia, he pursued a life of strict asceticism on Mount Maionis, enduring trials from demons, wild animals, and robbers.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable-Martyr Adrian of Poshekhonye
Also Known As: Adrian of Poshekhonye; Adrian of Yaroslavl
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Venerable-Martyr Adrian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born at Rostov the Great to pious parents, he became a monk and founded a monastery in the Poshekhonye region. He was slain by robbers at the end of the sixteenth century.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint John the New Martyr of Bulgaria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born in Bulgaria in 1775, he refused to renounce Christ and accept Islam, and was put to death for his confession of the faith.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: 42 Martyrs of Ammoria
Also Known As: 42 Martyrs of Amorion; Theodore Krateros; Aetios; Theophilos; Constantine Baboutzikos; Bassoes; Kallistos; Constantine
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs of Ammoria, pray to God for us.
