Brief Life: Byzantine generals and officials captured by the Saracens at the fall of the city of Amorion in 838. After seven years of imprisonment they were put to death for refusing to renounce Christ for Islam.
Notes: Named as one commemoration; seven of the forty-two are known by name.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Hieromartyr Conon and his son Conon
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Conon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A widower of Iconium who took up monastic life together with his son, also named Conon. Renowned for casting out demons, healing the sick, and preaching Christ among the pagans, father and son were both martyred for the faith.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Arcadius of Cyprus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Arcadius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of Cyprus from his youth during the reign of Constantine the Great. He was the spiritual teacher of the holy martyrs Julian the Physician and Eubolos, who suffered under Julian the Apostate.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyrs of Cherson
Also Known As: Basil of Cherson; Ephraim of Cherson; Capito of Cherson; Eugene of Cherson; Aetherius of Cherson; Elpidius of Cherson; Agathodorus of Cherson
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyrs of Cherson, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A group of seven bishops who carried the Gospel into the North Black Sea region around Cherson in the Crimea and were martyred for their preaching of Christ.
Notes: Named cluster commemorated together; individual names recorded in Also Known As.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Paul the Simple
Life Experience: Marriage; Difficult Marriage; Repentance from grave sin
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Paul, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An elderly Egyptian farmer who, after discovering his wife's infidelity, left the world to become a disciple of St. Anthony the Great; he was called the Simple for his guilelessness and gentleness of heart.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Paul the Confessor, Bishop of Plousias
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Paul, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Plousias in Bithynia who defended the veneration of holy icons from Scripture during the iconoclast persecution and suffered for his confession of the faith.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Emilian of Rome
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Emilian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born Victorinus, he lived a sinful life into old age before repenting, entering the monastic life under the name Emilian, and serving God in humility for the rest of his days.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Theophylactus of Nicomedia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Theophylactus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A 9th-century bishop of Nicomedia known for his charity to the poor, the sick, and lepers, building churches and hospices. For defending the veneration of icons under the iconoclast emperor Leo the Armenian he was exiled, dying in banishment.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Lazarus of Murom
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Lazarus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Greek monk who came to Rus' and founded monastic life on Murom (Mursky) Island in Lake Onega in the far north, enduring great hardship among the local peoples. He reposed in 1391.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Athanasius of Murom
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Athanasius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An abbot who labored at the Murom (Mursky) monastery founded by Saint Lazarus; his chains were long preserved as relics. He is commemorated together with Saint Lazarus.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hermes of the Seventy
Also Known As: Hermas; Apostle Hermes; Hermes of Dalmatia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostle Hermes, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: One of the Seventy Apostles, mentioned by Saint Paul in his Epistle to the Romans. He is said to have served as bishop in Dalmatia.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Theodoritus of Antioch
Also Known As: Theodoret of Antioch; Theodoritus the Presbyter
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Theodoritus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A presbyter and keeper of the sacred vessels of the cathedral church of Antioch built by Constantine the Great. Under Julian the Apostate he was seized, tortured, and martyred for refusing to surrender the church's treasures.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Felix of Burgundy
Also Known As: Felix of Dunwich; Felix, Bishop of the East Angles; Felix of East Anglia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Felix, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A 7th-century Burgundian-Frankish missionary who became first bishop of the East Angles, establishing his see at Dommoc (Dunwich). He converted King Sigebert and helped found a school for the kingdom, evangelizing the region until his repose around 647.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: 40 Holy Martyrs of Sebaste
Also Known As: Kyrion; Candidus; Domnos; Hesychius; Heraclius; Smaragdus; Eunoicus; Valens; Vivianus; Claudius; Priscus; Theodulus; Eutyches; John; Xanthias; Helianus; Sisinius; Cyril; Aglaius; Aetius; Flavius; Acacius; Ekdikios; Lysimachus; Alexander; Elias; Gorgonius; Theophilus; Dometian; Gaius; Leontius; Athanasius; Cyril; Sacerdon; Nicholas; Valerius; Philoctimon; Severian; Chudion; Meliton
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Forty Martyrs of Sebaste, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Forty Roman soldiers of the Twelfth Legion at Sebaste in Lesser Armenia who refused to renounce Christ and were condemned to freeze to death overnight on a frozen lake. When one fell away and a guard named Aglaius took his place, the number of forty was restored, and they are honored among the Church's most beloved martyrs.
Notes: One row for the named cluster per grouping rules; representative individual names in Also Known As.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Martyr Urpasianus of Nicomedia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Urpasianus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A dignitary of Nicomedia who renounced his rank and confessed Christ during the persecution under Maximian, and was tortured and martyred for his faith.
Notes: Suffered c. 295-305 under Maximian Galerius.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Righteous Caesarius of Nazianzus
Also Known As: Caesarius, brother of St. Gregory the Theologian
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Caesarius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The younger brother of St. Gregory the Theologian, an esteemed physician trained in Alexandria who served at the imperial court yet held to his faith. He received baptism near the end of his life and reposed shortly after, around 368.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Righteous Tarasius of Liconium
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Tarasius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A righteous man of Liconium commemorated on this day; little of his life survives.
Notes: Honest stub; biographical detail not preserved in the source.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Vitalius of Castronovo
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Vitalius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born to wealthy parents in Sicily, he embraced the monastic life from his youth and is venerated as a venerable father of the pre-schism West.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Quadratus and Companions at Corinth
Also Known As: Codratus; Cyprian; Dionysius; Anectus; Paul; Crescens
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Quadratus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Quadratus was born near Corinth to a Christian mother who fled to the mountains during persecution. Living as a hermit and healer, he and his companions were arrested under the Decian persecution, dragged through the city, and beheaded for confessing Christ.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Martyr Quadratus at Nicomedia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Quadratus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Quadratus came from a distinguished family and used his wealth to support and visit Christians imprisoned during the persecutions of Decius and Valerian. He and his companions were martyred at Nicomedia.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Anastasia the Patrician of Alexandria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Anastasia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A noblewoman of Constantinople in the reign of Justinian, Anastasia withdrew from the imperial court to escape worldly entanglement and the emperor's attentions. She lived in seclusion near Alexandria, eventually disguising herself as a monk and spending her remaining years in a desert cell.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint John of Khakhuli the Oqropiri
Also Known As: John of Khakhuli; John Chrysostom of Khakhuli; Ioane Okropiri
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Khakhuli Monastery in the Tao region of Georgia, John was renowned for his holiness and learning, earning the epithet Oqropiri (Golden-mouthed) after St John Chrysostom.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Michael the New Martyr
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Michael, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A New Martyr commemorated on this day; the surviving account preserves little beyond his name and martyrdom for the faith.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Sophronius of Jerusalem
Also Known As: Sophronius the Wise; Sophronius the Sophist
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Sophronius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born in Damascus around 560 and known for his learning and love of philosophy, he became Patriarch of Jerusalem and defended Orthodoxy against the Monothelite heresy. He led the Church through the Persian and Arab conquests of the Holy Land.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Euthymius of Novgorod
Also Known As: Euthymius II of Novgorod; John; Ioann
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Euthymius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born to pious parents who dedicated him to God and given the baptismal name John, he became a monk and was elected Archbishop of Novgorod in 1429. He was a notable builder of churches and patron of sacred arts, and his relics were found incorrupt.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Pionius of Smyrna
Also Known As: Hieromartyr Pionius; Limnus; Asclepiades; Macedonia; Sabina
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Pionius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest of Smyrna who, with his companions, suffered martyrdom during the persecution under the Emperor Decius. He boldly confessed Christ before the authorities and was put to death for the faith.
Notes: Commemorated together with Limnus, Asclepiades, Macedonia, and Sabina.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Sophronius of Vratsa
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Sophronius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born Stoyko Vladislavov in Kotel, he became Bishop of Vratsa and a leading figure of the Bulgarian National Revival. He composed sermons and an autobiography that helped shape the modern Bulgarian literary language.
Notes: Canonized by the Bulgarian Orthodox Church in 1964.
Works by the Saint: Nedelnik
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Sophronius the Recluse of the Kiev Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Sophronius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A recluse of the Far Caves of the Kiev Monastery who lived in great austerity, wearing a hairshirt and an iron belt and reciting the entire Psalter each day. He is numbered among the venerable fathers of the Kiev Caves.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Theophanes the Confessor of Sigriane
Also Known As: Theophanes the Confessor; Theophanes the Chronicler
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Theophanes, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A nobleman of Constantinople who left married life by mutual consent to become a monk and abbot at Sigriane. He suffered imprisonment and exile for venerating the holy icons during the second iconoclast persecution, and is remembered as the author of an important Byzantine chronicle.
Works by the Saint: Chronographia
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Demetrius Tavdadebuli King of Georgia
Also Known As: Demetre I of Georgia; Demetrius the Self-Sacrificing
Short Prayer (Intercession): Right-believing King Demetrius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: King of Georgia who, after a turbulent reign, willingly laid down his crown to embrace monastic life, taking the name Damian. He is remembered as a hymnographer and for his self-renunciation, by which he earned the title Tavdadebuli, 'the self-sacrificing.'
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Kirion II Catholicos-Patriarch of All Georgia
Also Known As: Kyrion II; Kirion II of Georgia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Kirion, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A learned bishop and church historian who became the first Catholicos-Patriarch of Georgia after the restoration of the Georgian Church's independence in 1917. He was found murdered at Martqopi Monastery in 1918 under circumstances that remain unexplained.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Martyr Sabinus of Egypt
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Sabinus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian of Egypt, an administrator of the city of Hermopolis, who was betrayed while in hiding during the persecution under Diocletian and, after torture, was drowned in the Nile for refusing to deny Christ.
Notes: OCA commemorates Martyr Sabinus of Egypt on both Mar 13 and Mar 16 (Sabinus of Hermopolis); merged here as one saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Africanus, Publius, and Terence of Carthage
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Africanus, Publius, and Terence, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A group of Christians at Carthage who, refusing to sacrifice to idols, were tortured and put to death for confessing Christ.
Notes: Also commemorated on April 10.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Publius, Bishop of Athens
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Publius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of Athens, counted by the synaxarion among the Seventy and successor to Dionysius the Areopagite, who shepherded the Athenian church and was martyred for the faith.
Notes: OCA counts him among the Seventy; some Western sources identify him with Publius of Malta and place his feast on Jan 22.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Martyr Alexander of Pydna
Also Known As: Alexander of Macedonia; Alexander the Presbyter
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Alexander, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A presbyter near Thessalonica who turned many from idolatry by his preaching and was beheaded after enduring tortures under the emperor Maximian Galerius.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Christina of Persia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Christina, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian woman in Persia who was scourged to death for her confession of Christ.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Benedict of Nursia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Benedict, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Benedict left Rome as a young man to live as a hermit at Subiaco, then gathered disciples and founded a series of monasteries, crowned by the great house at Monte Cassino. His Rule, balancing prayer, work, and obedience, became the foundation of Western monasticism.
Works by the Saint: The Rule of Saint Benedict
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Saint Theognostus, Metropolitan of Kiev and All Russia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Theognostus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Greek hierarch who succeeded Saint Peter as Metropolitan of Kiev and All Russia, ruling the Russian Church from Moscow through the mid-14th century and strengthening its ties with the Church of Constantinople.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Right-believing Great Prince Rostislav-Michael of Kiev
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Right-believing Prince Rostislav, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A son of Saint Mstislav the Great, Rostislav-Michael was a Great Prince of Kiev remembered as a devout ruler who supported the Church and worked for peace among the warring princes of his day.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Euschemon the Confessor, Bishop of Lampsacus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Euschemon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Lampsacus on the Hellespont, Euschemon was known for his ascetic life and defense of the holy icons, for which he suffered imprisonment under the iconoclast emperor Theophilus.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Agapius, Publius, Timolaus, Romulus, Dionysius, and Alexander of Caesarea
Also Known As: Agapius; Publius (Pausis); Timolaus; Romulus; Dionysius; Dionysius; Alexander; Alexander
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Agapius and your companions, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A group of young Christians who suffered together at Caesarea in Palestine during the persecution under Diocletian. Refusing to offer pagan sacrifice, they were tortured and beheaded for confessing Christ.
Notes: Named cluster commemorated as one: Agapius, Publius (Pausis), Timolaus, Romulus, two named Dionysius, and two named Alexander.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Alexander of Side
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Alexander, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest of Side in Pamphylia arrested and interrogated during the persecution under the emperor Aurelian. After enduring severe tortures for his confession of Christ, he was put to death.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Nicander of Egypt
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Nicander, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A physician in Egypt who ministered to imprisoned Christians during the persecution under Diocletian, bringing them food and reverently burying the bodies of the martyrs. He was seized and martyred for these works of mercy.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Papas of Lycaonia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Papas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian of Lycaonia who endured cruel tortures, including spiked boots, and was put to death at Diocaesarea during the reign of Maximian.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Serapion, Archbishop of Novgorod
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Serapion, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born near Moscow, he served as a parish priest, was widowed, and took monastic vows, later becoming abbot and eventually Archbishop of Novgorod.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Apostle Aristobulus of the Seventy, Bishop of Britain
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostle Aristobulus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: One of the Seventy Apostles and brother of the Apostle Barnabas, mentioned by St Paul in Romans 16:10. He is honored as a first preacher of the Gospel and a bishop in Britain.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Hieromartyr Alexander, Pope of Rome
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Alexander, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of Rome who guided the Church for about ten years and was martyred during the reign of the emperor Hadrian.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Julian of Anazarbus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Julian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian of Anazarbus in Cilicia who endured prolonged suffering and was martyred at Antioch; his relics became a source of healing.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyrs Trophimus and Thalus of Laodicea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyrs Trophimus and Thalus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two brothers and presbyters of Laodicea who confessed Christ and were put to death under the persecution of Diocletian.
Notes: Named pair of brothers commemorated together; one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saints Pimen of Salos and Anton of Meskheti
Also Known As: Pimen of Salosi; Pimen the Fool-for-Christ; Anton Meskhi; Anton of Meskheti
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Fathers Pimen and Anton, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Georgian ascetics who labored as missionaries among the peoples of the North Caucasus during the period of Mongol domination, Pimen taking on the podvig of folly for Christ.
Notes: Two missionaries commemorated together; one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Christodoulos the Wonderworker of Patmos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Christodoulos, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Byzantine monk and ascetic born near Nicaea who, after years in monasteries of the Holy Land, Bithynia, and Asia Minor, founded the Monastery of Saint John the Theologian on Patmos in 1088.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Saint Ambrose the Confessor of Georgia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Ambrose, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Georgian priest and later Catholicos-Patriarch who defended the independence of the Georgian Church and confessed the faith under Soviet persecution.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Alexis the Man of God
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Alexis, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born to a wealthy senatorial family in Rome, Alexis fled an arranged marriage on his wedding night to live as an unknown beggar, first in Edessa and later returning to live unrecognized as a poor man in his own father's house. He embraced voluntary poverty and homelessness for the sake of Christ.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Macarius of Kalyazin
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Macarius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A boyar's son born near Kashin in 1400, Macarius was married but embraced monastic life after the deaths of his parents and his wife. He founded the Holy Trinity Monastery at Kalyazin and was renowned as a wonderworker.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Marinus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Marinus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A zealous Christian during a time of persecution who destroyed the offerings the pagans were making to their idols and was put to death for his faith.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Gertrude of Nivelles
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Gertrude, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A 7th-century Frankish noblewoman who co-founded with her mother Itta the double monastery of Nivelles in present-day Belgium and served as its abbess, devoting herself to hospitality, care of the poor, and prayer. She is invoked by travelers and pilgrims.
Notes: Not on the OCA Mar 17 page; pre-schism Western saint with a Mar 17 feast. Region set to Gaul / France (no Belgium/Brabant term).

Name: Gabriel the Lesser
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Gabriel, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An 18th-century figure of the Georgian Church who received a good education and pursued a life dedicated to God.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives few biographical details.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Cyril, Archbishop of Jerusalem
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Cyril, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A native of Jerusalem who became a presbyter and then archbishop of the city around 350. He is renowned for his catechetical lectures instructing those preparing for baptism, and he endured repeated exile amid the Arian controversies.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Aninas the Wonderworker
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Aninas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A native of Chalcedon who entered the monastic life at the age of fifteen and withdrew to a life of extreme solitude in the desert near the Euphrates River, renowned for his ascetic feats and the gift of wonderworking.
Notes: OCA commemorates him on both Mar 13 and Mar 18.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Trophimus and Eucarpus of Nicomedia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Trophimus and Eucarpus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Soldiers at Nicomedia under the persecution of Diocletian who were charged with hunting down Christians, but who were converted through a vision and themselves embraced martyrdom for Christ.
Notes: Named pair commemorated together; one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Nikolai of Zhicha
Also Known As: Nikolai Velimirovic; Nicholas of Zhicha; the Serbian Chrysostom
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Nikolai, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Serbian bishop and prolific spiritual writer often called the Serbian Chrysostom, born in 1881. He was imprisoned in the Dachau concentration camp during the Second World War and later reposed in America in 1956. He is the author of the Prologue of Ohrid.
Notes: Glorified 2003. Also commemorated on the translation of his relics (some calendars May 3).
Works by the Saint: The Prologue of Ohrid; Prayers by the Lake
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Cyril of Astrakhan
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Cyril, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A hierarch associated with Astrakhan commemorated on this day; little biographical detail is available in the source.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA lists the name only with no life given.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Chrysanthus and Daria of Rome
Also Known As: Chrysanthus; Daria; Claudius the Tribune; Hilaria; Maurus; Jason; Diodorus the Presbyter; Marianus the Deacon
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Chrysanthus and Daria, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Chrysanthus, son of an Egyptian patrician family that had moved to Rome, embraced Christianity after seeking out the Scriptures. He was married to Daria, a former pagan priestess he had converted, and the two lived together in chastity; both were martyred at Rome along with many companions.
Notes: Martyred at Rome under Numerian, c. 283. Companions include the tribune Claudius and his wife Hilaria, their sons Maurus and Jason, the presbyter Diodorus, and the deacon Marianus.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Saint Innocent of Komel and Vologda
Also Known As: Innocent of Komel; Innocent of Vologda; Innocent Okhlyabinin
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Innocent, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Descended from the Moscow princely Okhlyabinin family, Innocent became a monk under Saint Cyril of White Lake and was a disciple of Saint Nilus of Sora. He later founded a hermitage in the Komel forest near Vologda and reposed in the early 16th century.
Notes: Disciple of Saint Nilus of Sora; reposed c. 1521.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Pancharius at Nicomedia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Pancharius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Pancharius, a friend of the emperor Diocletian, abandoned Christianity for paganism but was moved to repentance by a letter from his mother and sister. He openly confessed Christ again and was martyred at Nicomedia.
Notes: Martyred at Nicomedia under Diocletian.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Holy Fathers slain at the Monastery of St Sabbas the Sanctified
Also Known As: Martyrs John, Sergius, and Patrick of Mar Saba; Monk-Martyrs of the Lavra of Saint Sabbas
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Venerable-Martyrs of the Monastery of Saint Sabbas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A community of monks of the Great Lavra of Saint Sabbas the Sanctified near Jerusalem who were slain by Saracen raiders, refusing to abandon their monastery or their faith.
Notes: Named individuals include John, Sergius, and Patrick.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Euphrosynus of Blue Jay Lake (Sinozersky)
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable-Martyr Euphrosynus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Russian monk who, after time near Valaam, withdrew to a remote hermitage at Sinozero (Blue Jay Lake) in the Novgorod region, where he was slain during an invasion (d. 1612).
Notes: OCA dates him to the 16th c.; his repose (1612) places him in the early 17th c. (used here).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Virgin Martyrs Alexandra, Claudia, Euphrasia, Matrona, Juliana, Euphemia, and Theodora
Also Known As: Alexandra; Claudia; Euphrasia; Matrona; Juliana; Euphemia; Theodora
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Virgin-Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Seven Christian women martyred during the persecution under the emperor Maximian for their confession of Christ.
Notes: Named cluster commemorated together; one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Niketas the Confessor
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Niketas the Confessor, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Archbishop of Apollonias in Bithynia who defended the veneration of the holy icons during the iconoclast persecution and suffered for the faith.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Martyr Myron of Crete
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Myron, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A New Martyr of Crete who confessed Christ under Ottoman rule; few biographical details survive.
Notes: Honest stub; century not given by the source (low confidence).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: James the Confessor
Also Known As: James the Confessor, Bishop of Catania; Jacob the Confessor
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch James, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Studite monastery and a diligent student of Scripture, James endured persecution under the iconoclasts and confessed the veneration of the holy icons. He was later made Bishop of Catania in Sicily.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Cyril of Catania
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Cyril, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of the Apostle Peter who appointed him Bishop of Catania in Sicily. He was remembered as a wonderworker, including the healing of bitter water through prayer.
Notes: Originally from Antioch.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Thomas the Patriarch of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Thomas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Thomas served as deacon and sacristan under Patriarch John the Faster before being elevated to Patriarch of Constantinople; he guided the Church in the early seventh century.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Seraphim of Vyritsa
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Seraphim, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born Basil Muraviev, a successful merchant who, with his wife's blessing, embraced monastic life and was later ordained. As an elder at Vyritsa near Petrograd he became renowned for spiritual counsel and prayer through the years of Soviet persecution.
Notes: Glorified by the Russian Orthodox Church in 2000; his principal feast is Apr 3 (glorification). OCA lists him on Mar 21.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Serapion of Thmuis
Also Known As: Serapion the Sindonite; Serapion, Bishop of Thmuis
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Serapion, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Egyptian ascetic and companion of St Anthony the Great, known for his austerity. As Bishop of Thmuis in Lower Egypt he stood with St Athanasius against Arianism and wrote against the Manichaeans.
Notes: OCA merges the bishop with 'the Sindonite' ascetic; emitted as one commemoration.
Works by the Saint: On the Holy Spirit; Against the Manichaeans
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Enda of Aran
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Enda, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Once a warrior-king of Oriel in Ulster, Enda was converted through the influence of his sister St Fanchea and embraced the monastic life. He founded one of the earliest monasteries of Ireland on Inishmore in the Aran Islands.
Notes: Not on the OCA Mar 21 page (which lists 5); added as a pre-schism Western saint attested on the Mar 21 list.
